title: Structure-Guided Mixed Masked Pretraining and Spatial Continuity Regularization for Printed Circuit Board Defect Detection
summary: Printed circuit board (PCB) defect detection is an essential part of automated optical inspection (AOI); yet it remains challenging in practice because many defects are tiny, low-contrast, and embedded in dense circuit backgrounds. To address these issues, this paper presents a two-phase PCB defect detection framework that combines structure-guided mixed masked pretraining with spatial continuity regularization. In the pretraining stage, we design a sparse convolutional masked pretraining scheme to exploit unlabeled PCB images, where structure-guided mixed masking is used to construct informative masked inputs. The sparse convolutional reconstruction pipeline suppresses invalid responses from masked regions and enables the detector backbone to infer missing PCB structures from visible conductive patterns, thereby learning PCB structural priors. In the fine-tuning stage, the pretrained backbone is transferred to the downstream defect detection task. For the task, a spatial continuity regularization term is introduced during fine-tuning. This term constrains dispersed positive predictions assigned to the same defect instance and promotes more compact localization on elongated defect regions. Experiments on the DsPCBSD+ dataset show that the proposed method achieves 85.5% mAP0.5 and 52.3% mAP0.5:0.95, outperforming several strong baseline detectors. Ablation studies and qualitative results further confirm the effectiveness of the proposed framework for robust PCB defect detection in industrial AOI scenarios.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种两阶段PCB缺陷检测框架，结合结构引导的混合掩码预训练和空间连续性正则化。该方法首先利用无标签PCB图像进行稀疏卷积掩码预训练，通过结构引导的混合掩码构建信息丰富的掩码输入，使检测器 backbone 能够从可见导电模式推断缺失的 PCB 结构。在微调阶段引入空间连续性正则化项约束同一缺陷实例的分散正预测，促进紧凑定位。在 DsPCBSD+ 数据集上实现了 85.5% mAP0.5 和 52.3% mAP0.5:0.95 的性能。
AI.motivation: PCB缺陷检测是自动光学检测(AOI的重要组成部分，但在实际中面临挑战。许多缺陷微小、低对比度且嵌入在密集的电路背景中，难以被准确检测。
AI.method: 该方法采用两阶段框架：1) 预训练阶段设计稀疏卷积掩码预训练方案，使用结构引导的混合掩码构建信息掩码输入，稀疏卷积重建管道抑制掩码区域的无效响应，使backbone学习PCB结构先验；2) 微调阶段引入空间连续性正则化项约束分散的正预测，促进在细长缺陷区域上的紧凑定位。
AI.result: 在DsPCBSD+数据集上，该方法达到了85.5%的mAP0.5和52.3%的mAP0.5:0.95，优于多个强基线检测器。消融研究和定性结果进一步验证了该框架在工业AOI场景中进行稳健PCB缺陷检测的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该两阶段框架通过结构引导的混合掩码预训练有效学习了PCB结构先验知识，空间连续性正则化提升了缺陷定位的紧凑性，在公开数据集上取得了领先的检测性能，为工业AOI中的PCB缺陷检测提供了有效的解决方案。

title: Code-on-Graph: Iterative Programmatic Reasoning via Large Language Models on Knowledge Graphs
summary: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) are widely used to mitigate the limitations of Large Language Models (LLMs), such as outdated knowledge and hallucinations. Existing LLM-KG integration frameworks typically rely on predefined operators to retrieve factual knowledge from KGs and inject it into prompts for answer generation. This paradigm faces two critical bottlenecks: 1) Inflexibility: The predefined operators are limited in scope and thus lack sufficient compositional expressiveness to fully capture the complex semantics required by KG questions. 2) Unscalability: Direct injection of factual knowledge into prompts limits scalability in handling large-scale factual knowledge. To address these two bottlenecks, we propose Code-on-Graph (CoG), a programmatic reasoning framework for LLM-KG integration. Specifically, given the factual knowledge retrieved at each reasoning step, CoG first identifies the corresponding KG schemas and represents these schemas as Python classes, which serve as abstract interfaces to the retrieved facts. It then generates executable code grounded in these classes, with the retrieved facts instantiated as objects of the corresponding classes during execution. This design enables flexible code-based reasoning while avoiding the direct injection of large-scale factual knowledge into prompts. Experiments on WebQSP, CWQ, and GrailQA demonstrate that CoG outperforms prior state-of-the-art models by up to 10.5%.

AI.tldr: 提出了Code-on-Graph (CoG)框架，通过将知识图谱模式表示为Python类并生成可执行代码，实现灵活且可扩展的大型语言模型与知识图谱集成，解决传统方法的不灵活性和不可扩展性瓶颈。
AI.motivation: 解决现有LLM-KG集成框架的两个关键瓶颈：1)预定义操作符缺乏组合表达力，无法完全捕捉复杂语义；2)直接注入大量事实知识到提示中限制了可扩展性。
AI.method: 给定每个推理步骤检索的事实知识，CoG首识别相应KG模式并表示为Python类作为抽象接口，然后生成基于这些类的可执行代码，将检索事实实例化为类的对象进行执行，实现灵活的代码推理同时避免直接注入大规模知识。
AI.result: 在WebQSP、CWQ和GrailQA数据集上实验，CoG性能比之前最优模型提升高达10.5%。
AI.conclusion: CoG通过编程式推理方法成功解决了传统LLM-KG集成的不灵活性和不可扩展性问题，为大型语言模型与知识图谱的深度融合提供了新的有效范式。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about Large Language Models (LLMs) integrated with Knowledge Graphs for programmatic reasoning in NLP/software engineering domains. It has no connection to Computer Graphics, video-to-3D motion generation, cross-modal alignment between 2D video and 3D motion, or any of the methods mentioned in the profile (Video Generation Models, VLMs, 3D Motion Generation). The only superficial similarity is the word "Graph" but this refers to knowledge graphs, not motion graphics or 3D motion representation.

title: EvoMemNav: Efficient Self-Evolving Fine-Grained Memory for Zero-Shot Embodied Navigation
summary: Building memory is essential for long-horizon planning in zero-shot embodied navigation. Detector-centric scene graphs often compress observations into sparse nodes, discarding fine-grained visual evidence and accumulating noise, while 3D reconstruction-based methods remain computationally prohibitive. We present EvoMemNav, an efficient, self-evolving, fine-grained memory framework for zero-shot embodied navigation. EvoMemNav constructs a Visual-Semantic Memory Graph (VSMGraph) that keeps raw views as first-class memory and organizes them with lightweight semantic cues and topological relations into a room-view-object hierarchy, preserving fine-grained details for disambiguation and Stop verification. To scale to growing memory, we introduce a budgeted coarse-to-fine policy: a coarse stage compresses the search space into promising regions, and a fine stage invokes a VLM only for targeted verification and decision. Beyond static memories, EvoMemNav performs reflection-driven write-back after each subtask, updating graph-attached priors that encode accumulated environmental knowledge to refine future decisions without retraining. Experiments on GOAT-Bench and HM3D across object, text-description, and image-goal modalities show consistent gains in SR/SPL, with better multi-instance disambiguation, fewer premature stops, and stronger zero-shot generalization.

AI.tldr: EvoMemNav是一个针对零样本具身导航的高效自演进细粒度记忆框架，通过构建视觉-语义记忆图(VSMGraph)保留原始视角作为一等公民，结合粗到细的两阶段策略实现高效搜索，并在子任务后执行反思驱动的回写更新先验知识。
AI.motivation: 现有方法存在两大缺陷：1) 以检测器为中心的场景图将观察压缩为稀疏节点，丢弃细粒度视觉证据并累积噪声；2) 基于3D重建的方法计算成本过高。因此需要一种既能保留细粒度细节又能保持计算效率的记忆系统。
AI.method: 1) 构建VSMGraph：将原始视角作为一等公民，用轻量级语义线索和拓扑关系组织成"房间-视角-对象"层级结构；2) 采用预算化的粗到细策略：粗阶段压缩搜索空间到有前景的区域，细阶段仅调用VLM进行针对性验证和决策；3) 实现反思驱动的回写机制：每个子任务后更新图附先验知识，无需重训练即可 refined未来决策。
AI.result: 在GOAT-Bench和HM3D数据集上，针对对象、文本描述和图像目标三种模态的实验表明：SR(成功率)和SPL指标持续提升，多实例消歧能力增强，过早停止减少，零样本泛化能力更强。
AI.conclusion: EvoMemNav通过保留细粒度视觉细节的自演进记忆架构，配合高效的粗到细验证策略和反思式知识积累，为零样本具身导航提供了一种有效且可扩展的解决方案。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain. This paper is about embodied AI/robot navigation with memory systems, not Computer Graphics or video-to-motion generation. While both mention VLM (Visual Language Models), the usage is entirely different - the paper uses VLM for navigation verification, not for quality assessment in video generation. The core objectives are unrelated: one generates 3D motion from video, the other navigates agents in environments. No alignment with any of the four pain points mentioned.

title: Unveiling the Structure of Do-Calculus Reasoning via Derivation Graphs
summary: The do-calculus defines a general system of inference for interventional queries, allowing causal quantities to be transformed through successive applications of its rules. This process induces a rich space of equivalent interventional expressions, but combining and ordering these rules remains challenging. In this work, we introduce derivation graphs, which represent how do-calculus rules are applied and combined, and characterize the full space of observational and interventional probabilities which are equivalent under the do-calculus. The structure of these graphs yields a simple procedure that uses at most four applications of do-calculus rules. Finally, we show how applying identification algorithms to equivalent causal queries produces multiple valid estimands for the same causal quantity, eventually yielding more efficient estimators.

AI.tldr: 本文引入了导出图(derivation graphs)来表示do-calculus规则的应用和组合方式，并描述了在do-calculus下等价的观测和干预概率的完整空间。研究提出了一种最多使用四次do-calculus规则的简单程序，以及如何从多个等价的因果查询中产生更有效的估计量。
AI.motivation: do-calculus定义了干预查询的推理系统，但其规则的应用和组合顺序具有挑战性，需要一种系统性的方法来处理丰富的等价表达式空间。
AI.method: 本文引入导出图结构来表示do-calculus规则的应用和组合方式，通过对这些图结构的分析来得到最多四次规则应用的简单程序。
AI.result: 提出了一个最多使用四次do-calculus规则的简单程序，展示了如何从多个等价的因果查询中产生多个有效的估计量，从而获得更高效的估计。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入导出图结构，本文提供了一个系统性的框架来处理do-calculus规则的应用和组合，为因果推断中的识别和估计问题提供了更有效的方法。

relevance_reason: This paper is about do-calculus in causal inference theory - it has zero connection to Computer Graphics or video-to-motion generation. Completely different field entirely: causal reasoning vs. cross-modal video-to-3D motion generation. No overlap in application domain, methods, or research area.

title: Knowledge-Preserved Model Tuning in Null-Space for Robust Spatio-Temporal Video Grounding
summary: Spatio-Temporal Video Grounding aims to localize object tubes based on textual queries. While recent methods have achieved remarkable success, they mainly focus on high-quality(HQ) inputs, neglecting the widespread presence of low-quality(LQ) videos in real-world scenarios. Although tuning methods like LoRA can adapt to degraded inputs, they inevitably disrupt pre-trained knowledge. To address this, we propose Null-Space Tuning (NST). This framework exploits the geometric property that adding vectors within the null-space of frozen weights to the layer input does not affect the output. Leveraging this, NST injects learnable residuals into input features that can be selectively invisible to the pre-trained backbone. Specifically, NST combines the Quality-Adaptive Unit and Dual-Space Reparameterization to synthesize these residuals by confining components for HQ inputs to the null-space, while directing restoration components for LQ inputs to the non-null space. As the frozen weights eliminate null-space components, we effectively rectify degraded inputs while preserving pre-trained knowledge for HQ inputs. Extensive experiments show that NST outperforms state-of-the-art methods on our Mixed-Quality benchmark.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Null-Space Tuning (NST)框架，用于解决低质量视频的时空视频定位问题。该方法利用零空间的几何性质，将可学习残差注入输入特征，使高质量成分保持冻结，低质量成分得到恢复。
AI.motivation: 现有方法主要针对高质量视频，而忽视了现实中普遍存在的低质量视频。现有的LoRA等调优方法会破坏预训练知识，因此需要一种在不破坏预训练知识的情况下适应低质量输入的方法。
AI.method: 提出零空间调谐(NST)框架，结合质量自适应单元和双空间重参数化，将可学习残差注入输入特征。对于高质量输入，成分被限制在零空间从而被冻结权重消除；对于低质量输入，修复成分指向非零空间实现恢复。
AI.result: 在自建的Mixed-Quality基准数据集上实验表明，NST方法优于当前最先进的方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过利用零空间的几何性质，NST能够在修复低质量输入的同时保留高质量输入的预训练知识，为实际应用中的混合质量视频定位提供有效解决方案。

relevance_reason: This paper addresses video grounding (spatial-temporal localization in videos), which is a computer vision/video understanding task, NOT a video-to-motion generation task. While both deal with video, they are in completely different application domains: the research profile needs cross-modal generation from 2D video to 3D human motion (Computer Graphics/Motion Generation), whereas this paper focuses on understanding and localizing events within video frames. The methods and goals are fundamentally different—the paper doesn't address any of the specified pain points about video-to-motion conversion, temporal consistency, quality control for visual guidance, or semantic alignment. This is a video understanding task, not a motion generation task.

title: When to Re-Plan: Subgoal Persistence in Hierarchical Latent Reasoning
comment: Accepted at the Workshop on Compositional Learning: Safety, Interpretability, and Agents (CompLearn), ICML 2026. 10 pages, 2 figures
summary: Long-horizon reasoning requires a system to commit to medium-horizon intent without becoming rigid: re-plan too often and computation never coheres into multi-step structure; commit too long and the plan goes stale. We study this stability-adaptivity tradeoff in the latent reasoning setting, where multi-step computation occurs inside hidden state rather than externalized token traces. We extend the Hierarchical Reasoning Model (HRM) with a feudal-style manager-worker interface: a slow high-level module periodically emits a normalized directional subgoal that persists for P low-level steps, biasing the worker's hidden-state updates and supplying an intrinsic cosine alignment loss. On ARC and ConceptARC, we find that subgoal persistence -- not subgoal injection alone -- is the central knob: moderate periods P in [3, 6] consistently outperform both very frequent (P=1) and very long horizons, with a clear minimum LM loss at P=3 (1.544 vs. 1.674 at P=1, 1.640 baseline; replicated over 5 seeds at mean 1.595, std 0.045). The intrinsic alignment weight lambda shows a complementary narrow optimum (lambda approximately 0.05). A controlled ablation at past-sweet-spot lambda isolates learned directional structure -- not architectural capacity or auxiliary loss alone -- as the source of interference when the alignment signal exceeds its optimum. Together these findings implicate a design principle for compositional planning in latent reasoning systems: medium-horizon intent must be coherent across enough computational steps for compositional structure to form.

AI.tldr: 研究隐含推理中的稳定-适应权衡，通过在HRM模型中加入封建式管理器-工作者界面，周期性发出持续P步的子目标，发现中等持续期P∈[3,6]表现最优，其中P=3达到最低LM损失1.544。
AI.motivation: 研究者试图解决长程推理中"过度重规划导致计算无法凝聚成多步结构"与"过度坚持导致计划过时"之间的根本矛盾。特别关注隐含推理 setting——其中多步计算发生在隐藏状态而非外化令牌追踪中。
AI.method: 扩展HRM模型，加入封建式管理器-工作者接口：慢速高层模块周期性发出标准化方向性子目标，持续P个低层步骤，偏置工作者的隐藏状态更新，并提供内在余弦对齐损失。
AI.result: 子目标持续期P是关键；P∈[3,6]中等人群一致优于极频繁(P=1)和极长视野，P=3时LM损失最低(1.544 vs 1.674)；内在对齐权重λ存在窄最优区(λ≈0.05)；消融实验表明学习到的方向性结构是超过最优时干扰的来源。
AI.conclusion: 组合规划的设计原则：隐含推理系统中，中等视野意图必须在足够计算步骤上保持连贯，才能形成组合结构。

relevance_reason: Completely different field and application domain. The paper is about hierarchical latent reasoning in LLMs (AI/NLP domain), focusing on manager-worker interfaces for improving language model reasoning and planning. The research profile is in Computer Graphics, specifically about video-to-3D motion generation with cross-modal alignment between video and motion representations. Even though both involve "planning" or "reasoning," this paper addresses LLM reasoning (token-level computations), not any graphics or motion generation task. The methods are also completely unrelated - no diffusion models, video generation, or cross-modal pipelines involved.

title: Attend to Anything: Foundation Model for Unified Human Attention Modeling
summary: Existing human attention (saliency) modeling methods persist as highly fragmented across modalities, scenes, and task formulations. Consequently, even with increasing model capacity and data scale, current models predominantly remain scene-dependent and task-specific, failing to practically generalize in real-world applications. To address the fundamental limitations, we present the Attend to Anything Model (AAM), a multi-modal foundation model that unifies attention modeling across various image, video, and audio-visual tasks and scenes. AAM reformulates attention as a cognitive entailment relationship organized in a general-to-specific hierarchy, implemented through language prompts with hierarchical embeddings in hyperbolic space. Furthermore, to unify static image and dynamic video attention, we adopt a fluid-dynamics perspective, formulating video-frame attention as a diffusive temporal evolution governed by the Fokker--Planck equation. Extensive experiments on 16 benchmarks demonstrate that AAM consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods by an average of 6\% across various scenarios, while achieving approximately a 4$\times$ speedup in video inference. Overall, these results demonstrate that AAM provides a principled foundation for future research on attention and saliency-related tasks. The dataset and code will be available at https://github.com/wz-zhao/Attend-to-Anything.

AI.tldr: Summary generation failed
AI.motivation: Motivation analysis unavailable
AI.method: Method extraction failed
AI.result: Result analysis unavailable
AI.conclusion: Conclusion extraction failed

relevance_reason: This paper appears to focus on human attention modeling (likely eye-gaze prediction/visual attention estimation), which is a perceptual/computational vision topic unrelated to the research profile's focus on video-to-3D motion generation in computer graphics. While both involve humans as subjects, they address completely different application domains - "human attention modeling" is NOT "human motion generation." The methods and end goals are fundamentally different: the paper studies where humans look, while the research profile needs to generate 3D character motion from video inputs. This represents a misaligned application domain.

title: Proof-Refactor: Refactoring Generated Formal Proofs into Modular Artifacts
summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown strong performance in generating formal proofs, their outputs often remain less readable, modular, maintainable, and reusable than proofs in mature formal mathematics libraries. We argue that this gap stems in part from the compile-first objective implicit in most proof-generation pipelines, which encourages monolithic or ad hoc proof scripts rather than library-quality artifacts. Existing approaches to proof-quality improvement often rely on explicit, computable optimization objectives. In practice, however, the most tractable and experimentally validated objectives are largely length-based, while higher-level qualities such as readability, modularity, maintainability, and reusability are difficult to reduce to reliable automatic metrics. Instead of optimizing proof improvement against a single proxy metric, we take a process-guided approach inspired by human proof-refactoring workflows. We propose an agentic framework $\textbf{Proof-Refactor}$ that decomposes proof refactoring into four phases: extracting candidate proof fragments, designing helper declarations, formally proving the extracted and designed components, and repairing the original proof using the verified components. On generated Lean proofs from PutnamBench and Putnam2025, Proof-Refactor improves rubric-based refactoring scores over a strong Claude Code refactoring baseline, with the largest gains in signature quality and human readability. These results suggest that process-guided refactoring can improve proof structure without treating proof length as the primary objective.

AI.tldr: 提出了一个名为Proof-Refactor的智能框架,用于改进LLM生成的Lean形式证明的质量,将证明重构分为四个阶段:提取候选证明片段、设计辅助声明、形式化验证组件、使用验证组件修复原证明。
AI.motivation: 当前LLM生成的形式证明在可读性、模块化、可维护性和可重用性方面与成熟形式数学库中的证明存在差距,这一问题部分源于现有证明生成流水线中隐含的"编译优先"目标,它鼓励产生单体或临时性的证明脚本而非库级别的产物。
AI.method: 提出了一种过程引导的方法,包含四个阶段:(1)提取候选证明片段,(2)设计辅助声明,(3)形式化证明提取和设计的组件,(4)使用验证组件修复原证明。在PutnamBench和Putnam2025上生成的Lean证明上进行了实验评估。
AI.result: Proof-Refactor在基于评分标准的重构质量上优于强大的Claude Code重构基线,其中在签名质量和人类可读性方面提升最大。
AI.conclusion: 过程引导的重构方法可以在不以证明长度为主要目标的情况下改善证明结构,为自动证明质量提升提供了新思路。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant - this paper is about formal mathematical proofs and proof verification in Lean, focusing on refactoring LLM-generated proofs for better modularity and readability. The research profile is in Computer Graphics, specifically about video-to-motion conversion, cross-modal alignment between 2D video and 3D motion, and using methods like Seedance/VLM for 3D motion generation. These are entirely different application domains with no overlap.

title: \textsc{CR-Seg}: Attention-Guided and CoT-Enhanced Coarse-to-Refined Reasoning Segmentation
summary: Reasoning segmentation aims to segment target objects described by complex language through joint visual-textual reasoning. Existing methods typically rely on either learned semantic tokens to bridge Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) and segmentation models, suffering from difficult cross-modal alignment, or explicit spatial prompts such as bounding boxes, which may lose holistic response semantics. To address these limitations, we propose Attention-Guided and CoT-Enhanced Coarse-to-Refined Reasoning Segmentation, termed CR-Seg, a two-stage framework for coarse-to-refined reasoning segmentation. Specifically, we design an Extract Attention Maps and Points (EAP) module to extract attention maps for coarse target localization and select informative points, both of which are fed into SAM for mask refinement. To alleviate reasoning--answer inconsistency, we further introduce Global-to-Local Chain-of-Thought (GLCoT), which guides the model to reason progressively from global scene context to local target details. Extensive experiments on reasoning segmentation benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of CR-Seg.

AI.tldr: 本文提出CR-Seg框架，用于解决基于复杂语言描述的推理分割任务，通过粗到精两阶段方法结合注意力提取与思维链引导实现目标分割。
AI.motivation: 现有推理分割方法存在两个主要局限：一是依赖学习语义标记的方法存在跨模态对齐困难；二是使用边界框等显式空间提示会丢失整体响应语义。此外，还存在推理-答案不一致的问题。
AI.method: 提出两阶段粗到精推理分割框架CR-Seg，包含三个核心模块：1) EAP模块用于提取注意力图进行粗定位并选择信息点；2) 将注意力图和信息点输入SAM进行Mask精炼；3) GLCoT思维链引导从全局场景上下文到局部目标细节进行渐进式推理。
AI.result: 在推理分割基准数据集上进行了广泛实验，验证了CR-Seg的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过注意力引导和思维链增强的方式，成功解决了推理分割中的跨模态对齐和语义缺失问题，为多模态大语言模型与分割模型的结合提供了新思路。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain - paper is about image reasoning segmentation (CV), not video-to-motion generation (Computer Graphics). While both involve cross-modal alignment (language + images), the goal, input/output modalities, and downstream task are entirely different. The profile requires 2D video→3D motion conversion, semantic alignment between text and motion output, and temporal consistency, whereas this paper focuses on segmenting objects in static images based on natural language descriptions - a pure computer vision task unrelated to motion synthesis.

title: LAP: An Agent-to-Instrument Protocol for Autonomous Science
summary: Autonomous science is moving from demonstration to infrastructure. Large language model agents now plan experiments, and self-driving laboratories execute them. Yet every such system rebuilds the link between the reasoning agent and the physical instrument from scratch, against fragmented vendor SDKs and standards built for deterministic software clients rather than probabilistic, goal-directed agents. Recent agent-interoperability protocols clarify two of the three edges of an agentic ecosystem (Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardizes the agent-to-tool edge, and Google's Agent2Agent (A2A) the agent-to-agent edge), but neither models the agent-to-instrument edge, where operations are stateful, safety-critical, exclusively owned, physically embodied, and produce measurements with units, calibration, and uncertainty. We present the Lab Agent Protocol (LAP), a protocol design that fills this gap. LAP retains A2A's peer-to-peer, discovery-first, task-lifecycle structure and adds four physical-world primitives: (i) the InstrumentCard, a signed capability and physical-limit description; (ii) first-class reservation for exclusive instrument and sample locking; (iii) a safety-fence handshake with operator-confirmation tokens cryptographically bound to a specific task and its parameters, gating hazardous and irreversible operations; and (iv) a MeasurementResult schema that makes every result physically typed (QUDT/UCUM), calibration-anchored, uncertainty-bearing, and reproducible by construction. We specify roles, a six-layer architecture, the JSON-RPC method set, the task and safety state machines, the error model, and cross-laboratory federation, and walk a closed-loop autonomous campaign through the protocol end-to-end. LAP is transport-compatible with the A2A/MCP ecosystem and encapsulates rather than replaces existing device standards such as SiLA 2 and OPC-UA.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Lab Agent Protocol (LAP)，填补现有agent生态系统协议中缺失的agent-to-instrument边缘，设计了包含InstrumentCard、reservation、safety-fence handshake和MeasurementResult schema四个物理世界原语的协议规范。
AI.motivation: 当前自主科学系统虽能利用大语言模型agent规划实验并通过自驱动实验室执行，但每个系统都需从头重建推理agent与物理仪器之间的链接，且面临碎片化的vendor SDK和为确定性软件客户端设计的标准。MCP标准化了agent-to-tool边缘，A2A标准化了agent-to-agent边缘，但均未建模agent-to-instrument边缘——该边缘具有状态性、安全临界性、排他拥有性、物理具身性，并产生带有单位、校准和不确定性的测量结果。
AI.method: LAP保留A2A的对等式、发现优先、任务生命周期结构，引入四个物理世界原语：(i) InstrumentCard——带签名的能力和物理限制描述；(ii) 一级预留——排他仪器和样品锁定；(iii) 安全栅栏握手——操作员确认令牌密码绑定到特定任务及其参数，管控危险和不可逆操作；(iv) MeasurementResult模式—— физическиtyped(QUDT/UCUM)、校准锚定、带不确定性、结构化可复现。并指定角色、六层架构、JSON-RPC方法集、任务和安全状态机、错误模型、跨实验室联邦，演示闭环自主Campaign。LAP与A2A/MCP生态系统传输兼容，封装而非取代SiLA 2和OPC UA等既有设备标准。
AI.result: 本文规范了LAP的角色定义、六层架构、JSON-RPC方法集、任务和安全状态机、错误模型和跨实验室联邦，通过端到端协议演示了一个闭环自主Campaign的执行流程。LAP与A2A/MCP生态系统传输兼容，能够封装SiLA 2和OPC UA等既有设备标准而非取代之。
AI.conclusion: LAP成功填补了agentic生态系统中缺失的agent-to-instrument边缘，通过引入四个物理世界原语实现了对状态性、安全临界性、排他拥有性、物理具身性和测量不确定性的完整支持。该协议保留了A2A的peer-to-peer和discovery-first特性，与现有A2A/MCP生态系统及SiLA 2、OPC UA等设备标准兼容，为自主科学从演示走向基础设施提供了关键的协议基础。

relevance_reason: The paper is about Lab Agent Protocol (LAP) for autonomous science systems—completely unrelated to Computer Graphics. It deals with agent-to-instrument communication protocols for lab automation, not video generation, 3D motion generation, cross-modal alignment, or any computer graphics methods. There is no application domain match whatsoever.

title: Efficient Transformer-Based Localized Patch Sampling for Choroid Plexus Segmentation in Multiple Sclerosis
summary: Background: The lateral ventricle choroid plexus (LVCP) is gaining recognition as a key imaging biomarker for multiple sclerosis (MS) related to physical disability and neuroinflammation. Yet, manual segmentation of the LVCP is highly tedious, restricting its use in broad clinical trials and longitudinal assessments. This research aims to develop a SwinUNETR-driven pipeline that leverages targeted intra- and peri-ventricular small patch sampling to automatically segment the LVCP in MS from both standalone and multi-modal MRI inputs. Methods: We retrospectively assessed 3T MRI scans across three sets of data stemming from two separate MS-dominant cohorts (Dataset 1: n=177; Dataset 2: n=177; expanded test set: n=388). Our method employed a SwinUNETR architecture trained on 32x32x32 voxel patches, benchmarking it against the 3D UXNET model. The primary metric for evaluation was the Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC), supplemented by computational demand (GFLOPs) and the 95th percentile Hausdorff Distance (HD95). Results: On the extended test set, the SwinUNETR model secured a mean DSC of 0.868 (95% CI: 0.863-0.872) with MPRAGE and FLAIR combined, showing a statistically significant gain over UXNET (DSC: 0.858 [95% CI: 0.853-0.862], p<0.0001). When restricted to standalone FLAIR inputs, the transformer-based approach sustained a high DSC of 0.863, while the spatial localization of UXNET worsened considerably (HD95: 1.86 vs. 3.00 mm). Importantly, the proposed framework lowered computational load by 99% (91.8 vs. 22,080 GFLOPs). By integrating localized patch sampling with a SwinUNETR architecture, this methodology offers an accurate, robust, and statistically superior alternative to current leading models for LVCP segmentation. Its vast reduction in computational cost makes it ideal for widespread implementation in clinical and research environments.

AI.tldr: 研究开发了一种基于SwinUNETR的自动化管道，利用小 patch 采样技术对多发性硬化症(MS)患者的侧脑室脉络丛(LVCP)进行自动分割。该方法在三个数据集上进行了验证，在扩展测试集上使用MPRAGE和FLAIR组合获得了0.868的平均Dice相似系数，显著优于3D UXNET模型(0.858)，同时计算成本降低了99%。
AI.motivation: 目前LVCP作为MS相关物理残疾和神经炎症的关键影像学生物标志物受到越来越多的关注，但手动分割脉络丛非常繁琐，限制了其在大型临床试验和纵向评估中的应用。因此需要开发自动化的分割方法来解决这一瓶颈。
AI.method: 使用SwinUNETR架构，基于32×32×32体素的小块进行训练，采用针对性的室内和小周patch采样策略。评估了三种数据：两个MS主导队列的三个数据集（数据集1：n=177；数据集2：n=177；扩展测试集：n=388），并与3D UXNET模型进行比较。使用Dice相似系数(DSC)作为主要评价指标，补充使用计算量(GFLOPs)和95%豪斯多夫距离(HD95)。
AI.result: 在扩展测试集上，使用MPRAGE和FLAIR组合输入时，SwinUNETR模型的平均DSC为0.868（95%CI：0.863-0.872），相比UXNET的0.858（95%CI：0.853-0.862）有统计学显著提升(p<0.0001)。单独使用FLAIR输入时，transformer方法维持了0.863的高DSC，而UXNET的空间定位明显恶化（HD95：1.86 vs 3.00mm）。该框架将计算成本降低了99%（91.8 vs 22080 GFLOPs）。
AI.conclusion: 通过整合局部patch采样与SwinUNETR架构，该方法为LVCP分割提供了准确、稳健且统计上优于当前领先模型的替代方案。其计算成本的大幅降低使其非常适合在临床和研究环境中广泛推广应用。

relevance_reason: Completely different field and application. The research profile is in Computer Graphics focused on video-to-motion generation and cross-modal alignment between 2D video and 3D motion representations. The paper is about medical image segmentation (choroid plexus segmentation in MRI scans for Multiple Sclerosis patients). Even though the paper uses a transformer-based architecture (SwinUNETR), this is medical imaging/diagnostic AI, not computer graphics. The application domain, pain points, and research goals have zero overlap.

title: From Control Boundary to Insurance Claim: Reconstructing AI-Mediated Losses Through the CER Framework
summary:
AI losses that arise through an insured organization's generative or agentic AI system require state reconstruction, not merely event reconstruction, because the relevant state changes as the system reasons, retrieves, calls tools, and acts. The relevant question is not only what loss occurred, but what the system was allowed to do, what it actually did, and whether that reconstructed loss can support insurance claim recovery. This paper addresses losses in which the insured's AI system is in the causal chain, including externally triggered failures such as prompt injection, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) poisoning, malicious tool output, credential misuse, and data poisoning.
  Specifically, this paper introduces CER, a use-case-level diagnostic for AI residual risk transfer. C (control boundary) asks whether the system had an enforceable operating envelope. E (evidence reconstruction) asks whether the system state and causal chain can be reconstructed from retained artifacts. R (insurance response) asks whether the reconstructed loss is insured: whether insurance coverage is available in the market and placed for the insured, together with the proof needed to support insurance claim recovery.
  The paper makes three contributions: it defines the AI-specific reconstruction problem, operationalizes that problem through CER, and specifies claim-grade evidence for AI reconstruction. Public examples include the reported PocketOS and Replit agentic database-deletion incidents and Moffatt v. Air Canada as an adjudicated output/reliance case.
  Keywords: AI systems; CER framework; residual risk transfer; agentic AI; generative AI; AI insurance; evidence reconstruction.

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了生成式AI或代理AI系统导致的AI损失保险问题。由于AI系统在推理、检索、调用工具和行动过程中状态不断变化，传统的"事件重建"方法不足，需要采用"状态重建"来评估损失。作者提出了CER框架，用于诊断AI残余风险转移，包括控制边界(Control boundary)、证据重建(Evidence reconstruction)和保险响应(Insurance response)三个维度。
AI.motivation: 传统保险理赔通常基于"事件重建"，关注"发生了什么损失"。但AI系统具有自主推理和行动能力，其状态在整个运行过程中持续变化，简单的"事件重建"无法捕捉因果链的完整图景。作者认为需要重新思考AI损失的根本性质：不仅要问"发生了什么损失"，还要问"系统被允许做什么"、"系统实际做了什么"、"重建的损失是否能支持保险索赔"。
AI.method: 本文提出了CER框架作为AI残余风险转移的用例级诊断工具。C维度检查系统是否具有可执行的运营边界；E维度评估系统状态和因果链能否从保留的Artifacts中重建；R维度确定重建的损失是否有保险覆盖，市场是否提供该保险，投保人是否已投保，以及支持保险索赔所需的证据。论文还引用了PocketOS、Replit代理数据库删除事件及Moffitt v. Air Canada案例作为公开示例。
AI.result: 本研究定义了AI特异性的重建问题，将CER框架操作化，并明确了AI重建所需的索赔级证据。通过建立这一框架，能够系统性地评估AI系统在致损过程中的因果关系，判断损失是否可被重建，以及是否满足保险索赔的条件。这为AI保险产品的设计提供了理论基础。
AI.conclusion: AI系统造成的损失需要"状态重建"而非传统意义上的"事件重建"。CER框架为评估AI残余风险转移提供了系统性诊断工具，明确了控制边界、证据重建和保险响应三个关键维度。作者呼吁保险公司和AI系统使用者关注AI特异性的风险敞口，同时指出在AI保险市场中存在证据要求和可保性方面的挑战。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about AI insurance and risk assessment (using CER framework for "state reconstruction" to evaluate AI-mediated losses), which is entirely different from the Computer Graphics field focused on video-to-motion generation, cross-modal alignment, and visual quality assessment. There is no connection to video generation models, 3D motion generation, VLM for quality assessment, or any computer graphics methods.

title: Learned Non-Maximum Suppression for 3D Object Detection
comment: 6 pages, accepted at IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium (IV) 2026
summary: Post-processing is a critical stage in LiDAR-based 3D object detection, where dense and overlapping proposals must be filtered for compact and reliable perception. This work introduces two learned filtering modules that replace heuristic non-maximum suppression (NMS) by leveraging relations among detections. D2D-Rescore employs transformer-based detection-to-detection (D2D) attention, while GossipNet3D adapts the 2D GossipNet concept to 3D through localized message passing in bird's-eye view. A metric-aware matching strategy aligned with the nuScenes evaluation protocol ensures consistent training and validation behavior, improving overall detection performance. Both approaches improve mean average precision (mAP), nuScenes detection score (NDS), and true positive quality compared to CircleNMS, particularly for small and infrequent classes, while adding minimal computational overhead. These results demonstrate that learned, detection-level filtering can enhance 3D detector reliability without modifying the base network, offering a principled alternative to heuristic suppression. Code is available at https://github.com/rst-tu-dortmund/learned-3d-nms .

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种学习式过滤模块（D2D-Rescore和GossipNet3D）替代传统的启发式非极大值抑制（NMS），用于LiDAR 3D目标检测中的候选框后处理阶段，通过检测间的注意力机制或消息传递实现更紧凑可靠的感知结果。
AI.motivation: 现有3D目标检测方法依赖手工设计的NMS进行候选框筛选，存在对小物体和稀有类别召回不足、无法捕捉检测间关联信息的问题，亟需一种数据驱动且与评估指标对齐的后处理方案。
AI.method: 提出两种基于关系学习的过滤方法：1）D2D-Rescore采用Transformer架构构建检测到检测（D2D）的自注意力机制，重评分并筛选提案；2）GossipNet3D将2D图神经网络扩展到3D，在鸟瞰视角下进行局部消息传递。同时设计了匹配nuScenes评估协议的度量感知训练策略。
AI.result: 实验在nuScenes数据集上验证，两种方法均显著提升了mAP（平均精度）、NDS（nuScenes检测分数）和真阳性质量，相比CircleNMS尤其在小物体和稀有类别上改进明显，且仅引入少量计算开销。
AI.conclusion: 学习式的检测层过滤方法可在不修改基础网络的情况下增强3D检测器的可靠性，为传统启发式抑制提供了一种原则性的替代方案。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant application domain. This paper addresses LiDAR-based 3D object detection for autonomous driving, not computer graphics or video-to-motion generation. While it uses Transformer/D2D attention methods similar to some motion generation works, the application domain is fundamentally different - it's about perception and detection post-processing, not about generating 3D human motion from video or text. The pain points (cross-modal video-to-motion alignment, temporal consistency, text-to-motion semantic alignment) are entirely unrelated to 3D detection with NMS replacement.

title: Enhancing Operational Safety via Agentic Dialogue Hazard Identification Analysis
summary: Operational safety in high-stakes domains such as industrial process control, autonomous, and safety-critical systems, demand reliable hazard identification. While large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in automating safety analysis tasks, single-turn, monolithic inference is brittle: it lacks the self-correction, deliberation, and contextual refinement that safety engineers apply iteratively. In this paper, we introduce HAZDIAL, a framework that investigates whether structured agentic dialogue-multi-agent, multi-turn interactions improves the quality of NLP- based hazard identification over single-pass baselines. We systematically compare two dialogue modalities: adversarial debate and constructive discussion, and propose an algorithm-based agentic interaction optimization. We evaluate all configurations against a curated golden dataset using standard classification metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1) and novel dialogue metrics. This work advances the intersection of dialogue systems, multi-agent reasoning, and AI safety, providing an empirical evidence for dialogue-driven hazard analysis.

AI.tldr: HAZDIAL是一个研究多代理多轮对话是否能够改进基于NLP的危险识别质量的框架,通过对抗性辩论和建设性讨论两种对话模式与单次推理基线进行比较.
AI.motivation: 当前大规模语言模型在自动化安全分析任务中表现出潜力,但单次整体推理缺乏安全工程师应用的迭代式自我纠正、审议和情境细化能力,无法满足工业过程控制、自动驾驶等高风险领域的可靠危险识别需求.
AI.method: 提出两种对话模式(对抗性辩论和建设性讨论),设计基于算法的代理交互优化方案,在金标准数据集上使用标准分类指标(准确率、精确率、召回率、F1)和新型对话指标进行评估比较.
AI.result: 建立了一个精选的金标准数据集,系统比较了不同配置的系统性能,为对话驱动的危险分析提供了实证证据.
AI.conclusion: 该工作推进了对话系统、多代理推理和AI安全的交叉研究,为高风险领域的安全分析提供了新的方法论参考。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about multi-agent NLP dialogue systems for industrial safety hazard identification, not Computer Graphics. It has nothing to do with video-to-motion conversion, 3D motion generation, cross-modal alignment between video and 3D motion, or any of the video generation models mentioned in the research profile. The application domain (industrial safety/NLP) is entirely different from Computer Graphics (video-to-motion synthesis).

title: When Attention Collapses: Stage-Aware Visual Token Pruning from Structure to Semantics
summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities but suffer from significant computational overhead during inference. While visual token pruning offers a promising solution, existing methods predominantly rely on initial attention scores. This single-metric paradigm presents a critical flaw: high attention scores inherently collapse onto semantically similar regions, thereby severely reducing feature diversity and discarding vital contextual details. To address this, we introduce Structure-to-Semantics (STS), a novel two-stage visual token pruning framework that explicitly decouples the pruning process. The first stage employs a repulsion-based sampling mechanism to maximize spatial and structural diversity. The second stage leverages instruction-aware cross-attention to precisely filter out prompt-irrelevant tokens. This two-stage synergy constitutes the core of STS, first ensuring geometric coverage and then refining the retained tokens according to semantic relevance. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that STS mitigates the redundancy caused by attention-based selection, improving both structural diversity and fine-grained task alignment of the preserved visual tokens.

AI.tldr: 本文提出STS框架，一种用于视觉语言模型的两阶段视觉token剪枝方法，通过解耦剪枝过程解决传统方法中注意力分数过度集中导致特征多样性下降的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有的视觉token剪枝方法主要依赖初始注意力分数，但高注意力分数本质上会聚集在语义相似的区域，严重降低特征多样性并丢弃关键上下文信息，需要一种能同时保证结构多样性和语义相关性的剪枝方案。
AI.method: STS采用两阶段框架：第一阶段使用基于排斥的采样机制最大化空间和结构多样性；第二阶段利用指令感知的交叉注意力精确过滤与提示无关的token。两阶段协同确保几何覆盖后根据语义相关性精炼保留的token。
AI.result: STS缓解了基于注意力选择带来的冗余问题，提升了保留token的结构多样性和细粒度任务对齐能力。
AI.conclusion: STS框架通过显式解耦剪枝过程，为VLM推理阶段的计算效率优化提供了一种有效方案，在保持结构多样性的同时实现了语义相关的精准剪枝。

relevance_reason: While this paper deals with visual token pruning and VLM efficiency, it targets a completely different application domain - optimizing Vision-Language Models for multimodal tasks, NOT Computer Graphics or video-to-motion generation. The research profile focuses on 3D motion synthesis from video data, while this paper is about reducing computational overhead in VLMs through attention-based token selection. Even though both involve visual features, they address fundamentally different problems: visual understanding vs. motion generation.

title: Leveraging BART to Assess CS1 C++ Programming Assignments using Rubric-based Criteria
summary: This paper investigates rubric-aware, multitask fine-tuning of transformer models for automated grading of introductory C++ programming assignments, with the goal of producing grade predictions that better reflect instructor grading behavior than general-purpose LLMs. Using multi-semester CS1 data, student submissions are paired with numeric scores, letter-grade buckets, and assignment rubrics, then preprocessed into unified sequences for transformer input. A BART encoder-decoder with LoRA adaptation is trained to jointly predict numeric grades and grade buckets, augmented with a distribution-matching term to align predicted and empirical grade distributions, an evaluation dimension often overlooked in prior work. Experiments compare single-task and multitask training, hard one-hot versus fuzzy and boundary-based soft labels, and rubric versus no-rubric conditions, with additional T5 and pairwise-pretrained variants. Results show that multitask BART with boundary-based soft labels and rubric context achieves lower mean absolute error and stronger grade-distribution alignment than single-task, hard-label, or code-only baselines. Fully fine-tuned T5 further improves distributional fidelity, while pairwise pretraining reduces numeric error at the cost of minority-class sensitivity. Collectively, the findings suggest that calibration-aware, rubric-guided training produces more instructor-like grading behavior than accuracy-optimized alternatives.

AI.tldr: 本文研究使用BART编码器-解码器结合LoRA适配器对C++编程作业进行自动评分的多任务微调，通过引入分布匹配项和边界软标签来提高分数预测的准确性和分布对齐度。
AI.motivation: 现有通用LLM在自动评分时无法很好地反映 instructor 的评分行为，且缺乏对预测分数分布与真实分数分布对齐的关注。
AI.method: 使用BART编码器-解码器配合LoRA适配器进行多任务训练，同时预测数值分数和字母等级桶，并加入分布匹配损失项。实验比较了单任务/多任务、硬标签/软标签、有 rubric/无 rubric 等条件。
AI.result: 多任务 BART 结合边界软标签和 rubric 上下文达到了更低的MAE和更强的分数分布对齐；完全微调的T5进一步提升分布保真度；成对预训练降低了数值误差但牺牲了少数类别敏感性。
AI.conclusion: 校准感知、rubric引导的训练比纯优化精度的方案能产生更符合 instructor 评分行为的自动评分模型。

relevance_reason: Entirely different application domain. The paper is about automated programming assignment grading (educational technology), while the research profile is about Computer Graphics specifically for video-to-motion synthesis and 3D motion generation. Even though both may use similar neural network components, the use cases are completely unrelated - this would be like asking for help with image classification when you need motion generation.

title: Eliciting Complex Spatial Reasoning in MLLMs through Wide-Baseline Matching
comment: CVPR 2026. Project page: https://aim-uofa.github.io/reasonmatch/ Code: https://github.com/aim-uofa/ReasonMatch
summary: Wide-baseline matching (WBM) requires integrating geometric understanding, viewpoint changes, fine-grained perception, and occlusion reasoning, making it a challenging testbed for spatial reasoning in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) deployed in physical environments. However, current MLLMs lack systematic evaluation and training frameworks for these capabilities. We introduce ReasonMatch-Bench, a benchmark stratified by viewpoint displacement and matching granularity across indoor, outdoor, and object-centric scenarios, and show that current MLLMs still struggle with fine-grained wide-baseline correspondence: on a difficult 90-sample subset, human annotators achieve 84.0 F1, while the best existing baseline reaches 37.2. To bridge this gap, we build a scalable data-generation pipeline that automatically extracts wide-baseline view pairs from large-scale video-3D corpora, including RGB-D videos and SfM reconstructions, yielding diverse and verifiable supervision. We further propose Dynamic Correspondence Reinforcement Learning (DCRL), which combines Image-Level Viewpoint Progression and Point-Level Correspondence Curriculum to improve WBM training through verifiable rewards without explicit CoT supervision. Extensive experiments show that DCRL substantially improves ReasonMatch-Bench and transfers to related spatial benchmarks, while maintaining general visual understanding performance with modest gains on several benchmarks.

AI.tldr: 提出了ReasonMatch-Bench基准测试，用于评估多模态大语言模型(MLLMs)在宽基线匹配任务中的空间推理能力，并提出动态对应强化学习(DCRL)方法来提升模型的细粒度宽基线对应能力。
AI.motivation: 当前MLLMs缺乏针对宽基线匹配的系统和评估框架，而WBM需要整合几何理解、视点变化、细粒度感知和遮挡推理能力，是物理环境中空间推理的重要测试。
AI.method: 构建了分层基准测试ReasonMatch-Bench，按视点位移和匹配粒度划分场景；设计了可扩展的数据生成管道从视频-3D语料库自动提取宽基线视图对；提出DCRL方法结合图像级视点进展和点级对应课程，通过可验证奖励进行训练。
AI.result: 在90样本困难子集上，人类标注者达到84.0 F1，而最佳现有基线仅为37.2。DCRL显著提升了ReasonMatch-Bench性能，并能转移到相关空间基准测试，同时保持通用视觉理解性能。
AI.conclusion: DCRL方法有效弥补了MLLMs在细粒度宽基线对应方面的能力差距，为物理环境中的空间推理训练提供了新范式。

relevance_reason: While both papers involve computer vision, the application domains are fundamentally different. This paper focuses on wide-baseline matching (matching corresponding points across viewpoints) and spatial reasoning evaluation for MLLMs - a computer vision task. The target research is specifically about video-to-3D-motion conversion in computer graphics, which includes temporal consistency, quality контроля and cross-modal alignment between 2D video and 3D motion. Although both may use similar foundational models (VLMs), the paper addresses geometric matching/understanding rather than motion generation or video-guided 3D animation. The methods are indirectly related but applied to different domains and end goals.

title: Calibrating Urban Traffic Simulation from Sparse Road Observations via Genetic Optimization
summary: Urban traffic simulation is a critical tool for infrastructure planning, including the placement of electric vehicle charging stations. However, realistic traffic simulation across many cities is hindered by two fundamental data limitations: detailed real-world traffic measurements are available for only a small fraction of road segments in most cities, and employment distribution data critical for modeling commuter traffic is rarely available at the resolution needed for simulation. This paper presents a genetic algorithm-based framework that directly addresses both limitations, calibrating urban traffic simulations from sparse road observations without requiring detailed job location data. Using the SUMO traffic simulation platform for Greensboro, North Carolina, our approach optimizes job distributions and gate-traffic parameters to align simulated traffic with a small sample of roads with known traffic-flow rates. We demonstrate that this approach produces simulated traffic that correlates well with real-world measurements, generalizes to road segments withheld from training, and produces job distributions that show promising qualitative agreement with census employment data despite never directly training on that employment data. This work demonstrates that realistic urban traffic simulation can be achieved from minimal real-world observations, offering a scalable and data-light approach to simulation calibration that reduces the barrier to deploying traffic models across diverse cities.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了一种基于遗传算法的框架，用于解决城市交通仿真中的数据限制问题，在仅有少量道路观测数据且缺乏详细就业分布数据的情况下，校准城市交通仿真模型。
AI.motivation: 现实城市交通仿真面临两个基本数据限制：1）大多数城市的道路中只有一小部分有详细的真实交通测量数据；2）用于建模通勤交通的关键就业分布数据很少以仿真所需的分辨率提供。这些限制阻碍了真实交通仿真在多个城市的部署。
AI.method: 使用SUMO交通仿真平台针对北卡罗来纳州格林斯伯勒市，提出了一种基于遗传算法的框架，该方法优化就业分布和交通闸道参数，使模拟交通与已知交通流量的少量道路样本相匹配，无需详细的就业位置数据。
AI.result: 该方法产生的模拟交通与真实世界测量结果高度相关，能够推广到 withheld from training 的道路段，并产生了与人口普查就业数据具有定性一致的就业分布，尽管从未直接在这些就业数据上进行训练。
AI.conclusion: 这项工作表明，即使从最小的真实世界观测也能实现逼真的城市交通仿真，提供了一种可扩展且数据轻量的仿真校准方法，降低了在不同城市部署交通模型的障碍。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about urban traffic simulation using genetic algorithms - a transportation engineering topic entirely different from Computer Graphics. It has no relationship to video generation, 3D motion generation, cross-modal video-to-motion alignment, or any of the methods (VLMs, diffusion models) in the research profile.

title: Diffusing in the Right Space: A Systematic Study of Latent Diffusability
summary: Latent diffusion models leverage visual tokenizers to compress images into latent spaces for efficient generative modeling. However, better reconstruction quality of a tokenizer does not necessarily translate into better generation quality, suggesting that latent representations should be evaluated not only by fidelity but also by their diffusability. Recent studies have proposed diverse explanations for diffusion-friendly latent spaces, including semantic separability, affine equivariance, distribution uniformity, spatial structure, spectral smoothness, and manifold continuity. Yet these properties are often validated on a limited set of tokenizers, leaving it unclear which factors are most predictive of downstream generation quality and whether such conclusions hold beyond the specific settings in which they are introduced. In this work, we conduct a systematic study of latent diffusability by training a large collection of tokenizers with diverse regularization strategies, architectures, and latent configurations, and evaluating them with multiple downstream diffusion backbones. Our analysis identifies several latent properties that consistently correlate with generation quality and exhibit strong generalization across experimental settings. Beyond existing metrics, we introduce Velocity Irreducible Variance (VIV), a measure of velocity ambiguity induced by trajectory crossings. Extensive experiments show that VIV is one of the most stable predictors of generation quality.

AI.tldr: 研究潜空间扩散性(latent diffusability)，系统性地评估不同tokenizer配置与训练策略对下游扩散生成质量的影响，并提出VIV指标来预测生成质量。
AI.motivation: 当前tokenizer主要关注重建质量，但高重建质量不一定带来好生成质量。现有研究提出了多种解释diffusion友好潜空间的理论，但缺乏在多样化设置下的系统验证。
AI.method: 训练大量不同正则化策略、架构和潜配置的tokenizer，在多个下游扩散 backbone上进行评估，系统分析潜空间属性与生成质量的相关性。
AI.result: 识别出多个在不同实验设置下稳定预测生成质量的潜空间属性，并引入VIV(Velocity Irreducible Variance)指标，该指标是预测生成质量最稳定的指标之一。
AI.conclusion: VIV可作为评估潜空间质量的有效指标，帮助设计更好的tokenizer以提升扩散模型的生成效果。

relevance_reason: The paper studies latent diffusability for diffusion models, which is a method-focused work on improving tokenizer configurations for image/video generation quality. However, the application domain is fundamentally different: the research profile focuses on Video-to-3D Motion generation in Computer Graphics (converting 2D video to 3D human motion), while this paper is about general latent space optimization for image generation. Neither the specific application (video-to-motion conversion) nor the cross-modal pipeline (VLM for quality assessment, 3D motion generation) matches. This is analogous to diffusion for image generation vs diffusion for human motion generation - they use similar methods but address completely different problems.

title: BigFinanceBench: A Workflow-Grounded Benchmark for Financial-Research Agents
summary: Financial-research answers are decision-relevant only when another analyst can audit how they were produced: which source was chosen, which period and accounting definition were used, which assumptions were made, and how the calculation was performed. Existing finance benchmarks largely evaluate isolated subskills or final answers, leaving the auditable derivation itself under-measured. We introduce BigFinanceBench, a 928-item expert-authored benchmark of open-ended financial-research tasks in which each item pairs a ground-truth reference answer with a point-weighted rubric that decomposes the derivation into independently checkable steps. BigFinanceBench is workflow-grounded in that it evaluates the full derivation rather than only the final output. Across 36,241 rubric points, the benchmark supports partial-credit evaluation and localization of failures across the analyst workflow. Evaluating ten current frontier and open-weight agents, we find substantial headroom: the best system reaches only 58.8% rubric score, final-answer accuracy is a useful but lossy proxy for derivation quality, and model capability varies non-uniformly across financial workflows.

AI.tldr: 金融研究AI评估新基准BigFinanceBench问世，聚焦可审计推导过程而非仅最终答案
AI.motivation: 现有金融基准仅评估孤立子技能或最终答案，无法验证分析师如何得出结论，缺乏对可审计推导过程本身的测量
AI.method: 构建928项开放式金融研究任务基准，每项包含真实参考答案和点加权评分规则，将推导分解为独立可检查步骤，支持部分信用评估
AI.result: 评测10个前沿及开源AI代理发现：最佳系统仅达58.8% rubric分数，最终答案准确率是推导质量的损耗性代理指标，模型能力在不同金融工作流程中非均匀分布
AI.conclusion: 当前AI系统存在实质性提升空间，金融研究任务的评估需从单纯最终答案转向完整的可审计推导过程

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about financial research AI agents and benchmarks (BigFinanceBench), which is entirely in the Finance domain. There is no connection to Computer Graphics, video-to-motion generation, cross-modal alignment, or any of the methods mentioned (video generation models, VLMs for quality assessment, 3D motion generation). The application domain is completely different - finance vs computer graphics/motion generation.

title: UnsOcc: 3D Semantic Occupancy Prediction in Unstructured Scene via Rendering Fusion
summary: Unstructured scenes present unique challenges for autonomous driving, as irregular obstacles and sparse scene layouts undermine the effectiveness of traditional perception methods such as 3D object detection. 3D semantic occupancy prediction has emerged as a prominent focus due to its ability to provide dense spatial representations by assigning semantic labels to individual voxels in 3D space. However, directly applying 3D semantic occupancy prediction to unstructured scenes remains challenging because scene sparsity hinders effective cross-modal fusion and the more severe long-tail distribution in these scenarios further degrades prediction performance. To validate the effectiveness of our approach, we construct a dedicated dataset of unstructured scenes collected from open-pit mines. Based on this, we propose UnsOcc, a multi-modal 3D semantic occupancy prediction framework that improves robustness in unstructured environments. At its core, we introduce a rendering-based fusion module, RenderFusion, which enhances cross-modal feature alignment through bidirectional rendering supervision. Furthermore, we propose GSRefinement, a detail-aware auxiliary supervision method based on Gaussian Splatting that projects sparse 3D occupancy predictions into dense 2D semantic segmentation maps, enabling effective supervision for long-tail categories. Extensive experiments on both the open-pit mine dataset and the nuScenes dataset demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches.

AI.tldr: 本文提出UnsOcc框架,针对非结构化场景(如露天矿)的3D语义占用预测问题,通过渲染融合模块和Gaussian Splatting辅助监督提升预测精度和鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 非结构化场景中不规则障碍物和稀疏场景布局破坏了传统3D目标检测方法的有效性,且场景稀疏性阻碍跨模态融合,长尾分布进一步降低预测性能。
AI.method: 提出RenderFusion渲染融合模块实现双向渲染监督增强跨模态特征对齐;提出GSRefinement基于Gaussian Splatting将稀疏3D占用预测投影为密集2D语义分割图,为长尾类别提供有效监督。
AI.result: 在自建露天矿数据集和nuScenes数据集上实验表明,该方法显著优于现有state-of-the-art方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过渲染融合和Gaussian Splatting细节感知监督,有效解决了非结构化场景中跨模态融合困难和长尾类别监督不足的问题,提升了3D语义占用预测的鲁棒性。

relevance_reason: Wrong application domain. The paper focuses on 3D semantic occupancy prediction for outdoor unstructured scenes (mining, autonomous driving), while the research profile needs video-to-3D human motion generation for computer graphics. Although both involve cross-modal alignment, this paper addresses scene geometry/perception (autonomous navigation) rather than character motion generation. Gaussian Splatting is used here for scene supervision, not for motion generation or video synthesis.

title: EvoDS: Self-Evolving Autonomous Data Science Agent with Skill Learning and Context Management
summary: Recent progress in Large Language Model (LLM) agents has enabled promising advances in automated data science. However, existing approaches remain fundamentally limited by their static action sets and lack of principled long-horizon context management, hindering their ability to accumulate reusable experience across tasks and operate reliably in multi-stage, iterative data science pipelines. To address these challenges, we introduce EvoDS, a self-evolving autonomous data science agent that learns to expand its skills and adaptively managing long-term context through agentic reinforcement learning. Specifically, EvoDS introduces two key strategies: (1) Autonomous Skill Acquisition (ASA) mechanism, which enables agents to synthesize, validate, and reuse executable skills; and (2) Adaptive Context Compression (ACC) strategy, which treats context management as a learned control problem rather than passive truncation. These strategies are orchestrated within a two-stage multi-agent training scheme, enabling EvoDS to autonomously improve over time. Theoretically, we prove that EvoDS's hierarchical design reduces tool-selection error, and its optimization objective aligns with an information bottleneck principle, ensuring efficient context use. Empirically, EvoDS outperforms state-of-the-art open-source data science agents by an average of 28.9% across four diverse benchmarks while eliminating out-of-token failures. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/usail-hkust/EvoDS.

AI.tldr: EvoDS是一个自进化的自主数据科学智能体，通过自主技能获取(ASA)和自适应上下文压缩(ACC)策略来解决现有LLM智能体在数据科学任务中的局限性，在四个基准测试中平均超越最先进开源数据科学智能体28.9%。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM智能体的静态动作集和缺乏长期上下文管理原则，阻碍了在多阶段迭代数据科学管道中累积可重用经验和在复杂任务中可靠运行的能力。
AI.method: 引入两个关键策略：1)自主技能获取(ASA)机制，使智能体能够合成、验证和重用可执行技能；2)自适应上下文压缩(ACC)策略，将上下文管理视为学习控制问题而非被动截断。这些策略在两阶段多智能体训练方案中进行编排。
AI.result: EvoDS在四个不同基准测试中平均优于最先进的开源数据科学智能体28.9%，同时消除了超出令牌限制的失败。
AI.conclusion: 理论上证明了层次设计降低了工具选择误差，优化目标与信息瓶颈原则对齐。在实践中展示了显著的性能提升，为自主数据科学智能体的发展提供了新的方向。

relevance_reason: This paper is about autonomous data science agents with skill learning and context management - completely outside the Computer Graphics domain. It has no connection to video generation, 3D motion generation, cross-modal alignment between video and motion, or any of the methods mentioned in the profile (Video Generation Models like Wanxiang/Seedance, VLMs for quality assessment, 3D Motion Generation). The application domain is entirely different (Data Science/AI Agents vs Computer Graphics/Motion & Video Generation).

title: World Models Meet Language Models: On the Complementarity of Concrete and Abstract Reasoning
summary: World models and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) provide complementary capabilities for predicting future outcomes from static visual observations. World models can generate concrete visual rollouts of possible futures, while MLLMs can reason abstractly over questions, goals, and rules. However, generated rollouts are stochastic and may be visually plausible but task-incorrect, making it necessary to determine when visual simulation is useful, whether a rollout is credible, and how it should influence the final answer. We formulate this problem as controlled concrete reasoning, where a model learns to invoke, verify, and integrate visual future simulation alongside abstract reasoning. To study this setting, we construct two human-verified benchmarks, VRQABench for controllable spatial lookahead and OpenWorldQA for open-domain physical prediction, and propose Privileged-Future On-Policy Self-Distillation (PF-OPSD). During training, PF-OPSD uses ground-truth future videos and answers only as teacher-side privileged context to evaluate on-policy concrete-reasoning trajectories, while the deployable student never observes true futures at test time. Experimental results show that PF-OPSD outperforms baseline by 10.6% and 10.9% on VRQABench and OpenWorldQA, respectively, while increasing robustness to noisy or conflicting rollouts. Our code and dataset are available at https://github.com/yczhou001/PF-OPSD.

AI.tldr: 提出了PF-OPSD方法，结合世界模型和多模态大语言模型进行受控具体推理，并在VRQABench和OpenWorldQA两个基准上验证了其有效性。
AI.motivation: 世界模型可以生成视觉rollout，但可能看起来逼真却任务错误，因此需要确定何时视觉模拟有用、rollout是否可信以及如何影响最终答案，这是一个关键的开放问题。
AI.method: 构建了VRQABench和OpenWorldQA两个人类验证基准，提出PF-OPSD方法，在训练时使用真实未来视频和答案作为教师侧特权上下文评估策略具体推理轨迹，而部署的学生在测试时无法观察真实未来。
AI.result: PF-OPSD在VRQABench上超越基线10.6%，在OpenWorldQA上超越10.9%，同时增强了对噪音或冲突rollout的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法能学习调用、验证和整合视觉未来模拟与抽象推理，提升任务正确性和对错误rollout的抵抗力。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain. This paper addresses visual question answering and reasoning in virtual environments using world models and LLMs. It is NOT in Computer Graphics nor does it address video-to-3D motion generation. The research profile focuses on converting 2D video to 3D motion with temporal consistency and semantic alignment, while this paper focuses on answering questions about simulated worlds and evaluating the trustworthiness of visual rollouts for QA tasks. Despite both involving video/world models, the goals and applications are entirely distinct.

title: PyraMathBench: Evaluating and Improving Mathematical Capability in Large Language Models
summary: Despite the pivotal role of numerical reasoning as the cornerstone of mathematical capabilities in large language models (LLMs) across applications, few benchmarks evaluate LLMs by integrating numerical processing and mathematical reasoning, hindering the interpretability of failures in math tasks. We introduce PyraMathBench, a comprehensive hierarchical benchmark with 32,505 questions derived from 7,404 math word problems, spanning 4 key cognitive aspects, 14 subcategories, and 2 modalities. Experiments reveal that LLMs' performance is severely compromised by inadequate numerical computation and weak handling of abstract numerical questions. To address this, we propose the Smart Optimization & Learning-based VErsatile module (SOLVE) and Interactive Relative Policy Optimization (IRPO), which enhance LLMs' numerical-mathematical synergy via efficient tool calls (fuzzy matching and low-quality call rejection). Comparative experiments show Qwen-2.5 achieves a 5.0 score improvement with SOLVE and IRPO training.

AI.tldr: PyraMathBench是一个综合性的层级基准测试，包含32,505个问题，源自7,404道数学应用题，用于评估大型语言模型的数值推理能力。
AI.motivation: 数值推理是数学能力的基石，但现有基准测试缺乏对数值处理和数学推理的整合，导致数学任务失败原因难以解释。
AI.method: 构建了包含4个关键认知方面、14个子类别和2种模态的综合基准测试PyraMathBench；提出SOLVE模块和IRPO训练方法，通过高效工具调用（模糊匹配和低质量调用拒绝）提升LLMs的数值-数学协同能力。
AI.result: 实验揭示LLMs的数值计算能力不足且处理抽象数值问题能力弱；Qwen-2.5结合SOLVE和IRPO训练后获得5.0分的提升。
AI.conclusion: 该基准测试和训练方法有效解决了LLMs在数值推理方面的核心问题，显著提升了模型的数学能力。

relevance_reason: This paper is about mathematical reasoning benchmarks for LLMs (PyraMathBench), which is completely outside the Computer Graphics domain. It has nothing to do with video generation, 3D motion generation, cross-modal alignment between video and motion, temporal consistency, or visual quality assessment. The application domain is NLP/Language Model research, not computer graphics or motion generation. No methods or pain points from the research profile are addressed.

title: SkelHCC: A Hyperbolic CLIP-Driven Cache Adaptation Framework for Skeleton-based One-Shot Action Recognition
summary: Skeleton-based action recognition aims to understand human behaviors from body joint sequences and is especially challenging in the one-shot setting, where only a single labeled exemplar is available for each novel action. A key challenge is learning representations that capture the hierarchical and compositional structure of human motion while aligning effectively with high-level action semantics under extreme data scarcity. Existing approaches, largely based on Euclidean embeddings and low-level motion cues, struggle to model the tree-like organization of skeleton data, limiting cross-modal alignment and generalization to unseen action categories. We propose SkelHCC, a unified skeleton hyperbolic CLIP-driven cache adaptation framework for one-shot skeleton-based action recognition. SkelHCC introduces an Explicitly Hierarchical Hyperbolic CLIP (EH-HCLIP) module that embeds skeleton sequences and action language into a shared hyperbolic space. By leveraging the negative curvature and exponential volume growth of hyperbolic geometry, EH-HCLIP naturally encodes the joint-part-body hierarchy of human anatomy and yields structurally consistent cross-modal representations. To support efficient one-shot adaptation, SkelHCC further integrates a training-free LLM-guided Multi-granularity Voting Cache (LMV-Cache) for context-aware inference. Experiments on NTU RGB+D 60, NTU RGB+D 120, and PKU-MMD demonstrate that SkelHCC consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods.

AI.tldr: SkelHCC是一个统一的基于骨架动作识别的单样本学习框架，利用双曲空间和CLIP模型实现跨模态对齐
AI.motivation: 单样本设置下仅有一个标注样本，学习能捕捉人体运动层级结构并与动作语义有效对齐的表示是核心挑战。现有欧几里得嵌入方法难以建模骨架数据的树状组织，限制了跨模态对齐和对未知动作类别的泛化。
AI.method: 提出EH-HCLIP模块将骨架序列和动作语言嵌入共享双曲空间，利用负曲率和指数体积增长特性编码人体关节-部分-身体的层级结构；同时集成训练无关的LLM引导多粒度投票缓存实现上下文感知的推理。
AI.result: 在NTU RGB+D 60、NTU RGB+D 120和PKU-MMD数据集上验证，SkelHCC一致优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 双曲几何和跨模态CLIP对齐为单样本骨架动作识别提供有效解决方案，通过显式层级双曲嵌入和LLM缓存适配突破了数据稀缺下的表示学习瓶颈。

relevance_reason: While this paper uses CLIP and deals with skeleton data (similar elements to the research profile), the core application domain is completely different: it's about skeleton-based ONE-SHOT ACTION RECOGNITION (classification), not video-to-motion GENERATION or text-to-motion generation. The research profile specifically needs methods for converting 2D video → 3D motion and maintaining semantic alignment between text prompts and 3D motion output. The paper addresses recognizing/classifying actions from skeleton sequences - a discriminative task rather than the generative task needed. Additionally, it uses skeleton sequences as input, not 2D video as required. The hyperbolic space approach for hierarchical structure is methodologically interesting but applied in a different problem domain.

title: Reasoning Structure of Large Language Models
comment: Accepted at ICML 2026 and presented at the ICLR 2026 workshop on LLM reasoning
summary: Large reasoning models (LRMs) are often evaluated using metrics such as final-answer accuracy or token count. However, identical scores on these metrics can hide fundamentally different reasoning structures. To address this limitation, we introduce a scalable LRM benchmark of logic puzzles and a pipeline that converts unstructured traces into verifiable reasoning graphs of claims and dependencies. This turns reasoning into a structured, measurable object whose topology can be quantitatively analyzed. Building on this, we define a reasoning efficiency metric that quantifies how concentrated the model's logical flow is. Our analysis on open-source reasoning models shows that structural measurements separate behaviors that token count and accuracy conflate, providing a practical tool for diagnosing failure modes and comparing how reasoning scales with puzzle difficulty.

AI.tldr: Summary generation failed
AI.motivation: Motivation analysis unavailable
AI.method: Method extraction failed
AI.result: Result analysis unavailable
AI.conclusion: Conclusion extraction failed

While LLMs might share some underlying architectural concepts (transformers), the application domain is fundamentally different - this is about language model reasoning, not video-to-motion generation, cross-modal alignment, or any of the specified computer graphics methods (Wanxiang/Seedance, VLM for quality assessment, 3D motion generation). The paper does not address any of the stated pain points regarding 2D video to 3D motion conversion.

title: TurtleAI: Benchmarking Multimodal Models for Visual Programming in Turtle Graphics
summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) have been explored for visual programming, where they generate code to solve visual tasks. However, most prior work focuses on visual programming for productivity; it remains unclear how well current VLMs perform on education-oriented visual programming and what factors limit their performance. To bridge this gap, we introduce TurtleAI, a benchmark containing 823 tasks curated based on real-world visual programming tasks in the Turtle Graphics domain. Solving these tasks requires models to perceive geometric patterns, reason about spatial relationships, and synthesize Python code that faithfully reproduces geometric patterns. We evaluate 20+ VLMs, including GPT-5, GPT-4o, and Qwen2-VL-72B, and find that they struggle significantly, with most achieving success rates below 30%. To address these limitations, we propose a data generation technique that requires only a small set of seed samples. Fine-tuning Qwen2-VL-72B on the resulting synthetic data yields an improvement of about 20% on real-world tasks. Our failure analysis reveals that GPT-4o struggles with spatial reasoning and precise visual replication, whereas fine-tuning primarily improves the alignment between visual reasoning and code implementation.

AI.tldr: TurtleAI是一个面向教育领域的视觉编程基准数据集，包含823个海龟图形（ Turtle Graphics）任务，用于评估视觉语言模型生成代码的能力。评估20+个主流VLM后发现大多数模型准确率低于30%，通过提出的数据生成技术对Qwen2-VL-72B进行微调可提升约20%的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉编程研究多聚焦于生产力应用，缺乏对教育-oriented视觉编程能力的探索。研究旨在填补这一空白，评估当前VLM在教育领域的表现及其局限性。
AI.method: 构建TurtleAI基准数据集（823个真实世界的Turtle Graphics任务），评估GPT-5、GPT-4o、Qwen2-VL-72B等20+个VLM，提出仅需少量种子样本的数据生成技术用于微调。
AI.result: 大多数VLM表现不佳，成功率低于30%；微调Qwen2-VL-72B后提升约20%。失败分析显示GPT-4o在空间推理和精确视觉复现方面存在困难。
AI.conclusion: 空间推理和精确视觉复现是限制当前VLM教育视觉编程能力的主要因素；微调主要改善了视觉推理与代码实现之间的对齐。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain. This paper is about educational visual programming (Turtle Graphics code generation), while the research profile focuses on video-to-3D motion generation with cross-modal alignment between generated videos and 3D motion. Although both mention VLM (Visual Language Models), this paper uses VLM for code generation assessment in education, not for quality assessment of generated video content or video-to-motion conversion. Turtle Graphics is about 2D geometric visualization/programming education, not 3D human motion generation.

title: scTranslation: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Single-Cell Multi-Omics Modality Translation
summary: Simultaneous measurement of multiple omics modalities in single cells enables researchers to gain a more comprehensive understanding of cellular states and regulatory mechanisms. However, due to high experimental costs, significant noise, and incomplete modality coverage, a variety of computational methods for modality translation have emerged in recent years. Despite the development of translation models, there is still a lack of systematic benchmark evaluation in terms of datasets, evaluation metrics, and influencing factors. To address this, we present scTranslation, a comprehensive benchmark for single-cell multi-omics modality translation tasks. It includes diverse translation datasets, integrates state-of-the-art models, and provides a comprehensive evaluation metrics. In addition, we assess model performance under different scenarios, such as feature selection, feature quality, and few-shot settings. These factors significantly affect model performance but have rarely been systematically studied before. Leveraging this benchmark, we conduct a large-scale study of current methods, report many insightful findings that open up new possibilities for future development. The benchmark is open-sourced to facilitate future research. The code is anonymously released at https://github.com/Bunnybeibei/scTranslation.

AI.tldr: 提出了scTranslation，一个单细胞多组学模态翻译任务的综合基准测试框架，解决了该领域缺乏系统化评估基准的问题。
AI.motivation: 单细胞多组学测量成本高、噪音大且模态覆盖不完整，虽然已出现多种计算模态翻译方法，但尚缺乏系统性的基准评估，包括数据集、评估指标和影响因素等方面。
AI.method: 构建了包含多样化翻译数据集的基准，整合了最先进的翻译模型，并提供全面的评估指标，还评估了在特征选择、特征质量和少样本设置等不同场景下的模型性能。
AI.result: 对当前方法进行了大规模研究，获得了许多有价值的发现，为未来的研究开辟了新的可能性，代码已在GitHub上开源。
AI.conclusion: scTranslation基准测试框架的提出为单细胞多组学模态翻译任务提供了标准化的评估平台，推动了该领域方法的系统性比较和发展。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. This paper is about single-cell multi-omics modality translation in computational biology/genomics, which is an entirely different field from Computer Graphics. There is no overlap with video-to-motion conversion, 3D motion generation, cross-modal alignment between 2D video and 3D motion, or any other aspect of the research profile. The application domain is fundamentally different - bioinformatics vs computer graphics.

title: VidMsg: A Benchmark for Implicit Message Inference in Short Videos
summary: Understanding short online videos involves more than identifying visible objects and actions; video makers often include an underlying message or purpose in the clip. We introduce VidMsg, a benchmark for evaluating implicit message understanding in short, internet-native video clips. VidMsg contains 400 YouTube-derived clips across 9 practical topic areas and 52 fine-grained target messages, covering domains such as career and finance, education, health and well-being, culture, safety, sustainability, and lifestyle. VidMsg is constructed through a message-first pipeline: an LLM first translates target messages into indirect search scenarios, which are used to retrieve candidate clips. Human annotators then retain clips that convey the intended message without being overly explicit. VidMsg is designed primarily for bidirectional message-clip retrieval for scalable applications such as video search and recommendation, where systems must capture holistic video understanding. In addition to retrieval, VidMsg includes a diagnostic multiple-choice QA benchmark, where models select the intended message of a clip from semantically related alternatives. Experiments with contemporary video-language and retrieval models show that strong models often fail on VidMsg, because the task requires pragmatic inference, integration of contextual cues, and discrimination among semantically close messages. We also introduce VidVec-Msg, a baseline method that improves message-oriented retrieval while leaving substantial headroom for future work.

AI.tldr: VidMsg是一个评估短视频中隐含信息理解能力的基准测试，包含400个YouTube视频片段，涵盖9个主题领域和52个细粒度目标消息。基准采用"消息优先"pipeline构建，通过LLM生成间接搜索场景来检索候选视频，再由人工标注筛选出能传达目标信息但不过于明确的片段。
AI.motivation: 理解短视频不仅需要识别可见的物体和动作，视频创作者通常会在片段中加入潜在的意图或目的。现有视觉语言模型在处理这类需要务实推理、整合语境线索的任务时表现不佳，因此需要一个新的benchmark来评估模型的隐含信息理解能力。
AI.method: 采用消息-first pipeline：(1)使用LLM将目标消息转换为间接搜索场景；(2)利用这些场景检索候选视频片段；(3)由人工标注者保留能传达意图信息但不过于直白的片段。同时设计了双向消息-视频检索任务和多选QA诊断基准。
AI.result: 当代视频语言和检索模型在VidMsg上经常失败，因为该任务需要务实的推理、整合语境线索以及区分语义相近的消息。VidVec-Msg基线方法有一定提升，但仍存在较大改进空间。
AI.conclusion: 隐含消息理解是短视频理解的重要组成部分，当前模型在需要 pragmatics inference 和语义区分的任务上仍有显著不足。VidMsg为相关研究和应用提供了有价值的评估基准和挑战。

relevance_reason: Complete mismatch in application domain. The research profile is about Computer Graphics - specifically video-to-motion conversion (converting 2D video to 3D human motion). The VidMsg paper is about video understanding and implicit message inference for evaluating VLMs, which falls under video/multimodal AI rather than graphics or 3D motion generation. While both deal with video, they address completely different problems: one is about extracting 3D motion data from video, the other is about inferring intended messages from video content. No alignment with any of the pain points or methods listed.

title: Hedge-Bench: Benchmarking Agents on Hard, Realistic Tasks Pertaining to Financial Reasoning
comment: Dataset and evaluation harness available at github.com/Trata-Inc/trata-hedge-bench
summary: AI agents can increasingly handle the mechanical tasks of financial analysis: retrieving documents, calculating formulas, updating spreadsheets. The harder, more valuable challenge is reasoning through the open-ended questions that define expert Analyst work. Existing benchmarks do not capture this class of problem, and those that attempt to evaluate open-ended reasoning rely on model-judged outputs that introduce noise and circularity. We present Hedge-Bench 1.0: a benchmark of 102 actual, on-the-job tasks grounded in the explicit reasoning traces of professional hedge fund analysts working with relevant information sources. This approach enables deterministic grading against verified expert steps. Frontier models and agents score below 16\% on the benchmark. We publish the dataset and evaluation harness at github.com/Trata-Inc/trata-hedge-bench.

AI.tldr: Hedge-Bench 1.0 是一个基准测试数据集，包含102个真实的、对冲基金分析师的实际工作任务，用于评估AI模型在开放性金融推理任务上的表现，现有前沿模型的得分低于16%。
AI.motivation: 现有基准测试无法捕捉专业分析师的开放式推理任务，而那些试图评估开放式推理的基准依赖模型评判输出，引入噪声和循环性问题，因此需要一个基于真实专业推理过程的新基准。
AI.method: 构建了一个包含102个实际工作任务的数据集，这些任务来源于专业对冲基金分析师的显式推理痕迹，并配备了相关信息源，使用确定性评分方法对照验证过的专家步骤进行评估。
AI.result: 前沿模型和代理在该基准测试上的得分均低于16%，表明当前AI系统在处理开放式金融推理任务方面仍有很大提升空间。
AI.conclusion: 该研究提供了一个开源的基准测试数据集和评估工具，为衡量和改进AI在专业金融分析推理方面的能力提供了重要参考，同时也揭示了现有AI系统的局限性。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant. The research profile is in Computer Graphics (video-to-motion conversion, 3D motion generation, cross-modal alignment), while this paper is about financial reasoning AI agents and hedge fund benchmarks. There is no overlap in application domain - they address entirely different fields (financial analysis vs computer graphics/motion generation).

title: A Benchmark for Semi-supervised Multi-modal Crowd Counting
summary: This paper constructs the first benchmark on semi-supervised multi-modal crowd counting. To lay the foundation for this unexplored task, we first formulate the semi-supervised multi-modal setting and a standardized protocol that specifies the labeled-unlabeled data partition across different labeled ratios. Next, to establish solid reference points, we carefully tailor a diverse set of representative baselines, including existing fully supervised multi-modal methods and semi-supervised single-modal methods. Then, we carefully evaluate their performance under our proposed benchmark. Codes and the data partition will be released on https://github.com/HenryCilence/Semi-supervised-Multimodal-Crowd-Counting.

AI.tldr: Summary generation failed
AI.motivation: Motivation analysis unavailable
AI.method: Method extraction failed
AI.result: Result analysis unavailable
