title: Bootstrap Your Generator: Unpaired Visual Editing with Flow Matching
comment: Accepted at ICML 2026. Project page is at https://research.nvidia.com/labs/par/byg/
summary: Modern generative models possess a deep understanding of visual content, yet training them for image editing typically requires massive datasets of paired examples. This limits scalability, especially for video editing where collecting paired data is prohibitively expensive. We propose Bootstrap Your Generator (ByG), a general framework for unpaired training of flow matching editing models. It leverages the base model's knowledge without any external signal. Our approach pairs instruction-following cues extracted from the frozen model with cycle-consistency for structure preservation. To make this tractable, we propose to route gradients from downstream losses over clean predictions to noisy training states. We demonstrate state-of-the-art results on challenging data-scarce image and video editing scenarios. Extensive evaluations and user studies show that our method effectively generalizes to unseen domains and outperforms supervised baselines trained on millions of samples. Analysis reveals that our gradient routing bridges the train-inference gap, and extracting semantic cues from a base model provides a robust training signal that obviates the need for external reward models.

AI.tldr: ByG是一种无需配对数据的图像/视频编辑模型训练框架，通过从冻结模型中提取指令-following线索并结合循环一致性来实现结构保持，无需外部奖励模型即可达到最先进的编辑效果。
AI.motivation: 当前生成模型训练需要大量配对数据，这在图像编辑特别是视频编辑中成本高昂，限制了可扩展性。现有方法依赖大规模监督数据，难以应用于数据稀缺的场景。
AI.method: 提出Bootstrap Your Generator框架，利用基础模型的内在知识作为监督信号。具体包括：1)从冻结模型中提取instruction-following语义线索；2)使用cycle-consistency保持结构；3)通过梯度路由将下游损失从干净预测传递到噪声训练状态，弥合训练与推理的差距。
AI.result: 在数据稀缺的图像和视频编辑场景中达到最先进水平。广泛的评估和用户研究表明，该方法能有效泛化到未见领域，在百万级样本监督基线的基础上进一步提升性能。
AI.conclusion: 梯度路由技术弥合了训练与推理的差距，从基础模型提取语义线索提供了强大的训练信号，消除了对外部奖励模型的需求，为低成本、高质量的生成式编辑开辟了新路径。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain. The research profile focuses on VIDEO-TO-MOTION GENERATION (converting 2D video into 3D human motion), while this paper is about IMAGE/VIDEO EDITING (modifying visual content). Although both involve video, the core task is fundamentally different - one generates motion from video, the other edits visual content. The paper addresses unpaired data training for editing but does not touch upon cross-modal video-to-motion alignment, 3D motion generation, temporal consistency for motion synthesis, or semantic text-motion alignment which are the core pain points. No overlap in application domain.

title: PatchScene: Patch-based Voxel Diffusion for Large-Scale Scene Completion
summary: We propose PatchScene, a novel diffusion-based framework for large-scale LiDAR scene completion. Unlike existing methods that rely on global latent representations or dense voxel grids, PatchScene adopts a patch-based voxel diffusion paradigm that explicitly generates fine-grained geometry within localized 3D regions. To ensure coherent reconstruction at both spatial and temporal scales, we introduce a confidence-guided spatio-temporal fusion mechanism that integrates overlapping patches and adjacent frames in a unified generative process. Furthermore, we design an Annular-Flow diffusion strategy that leverages the radial density pattern of LiDAR scans to progressively propagate high-fidelity information from near-range to far-range regions, enabling spatially unbounded scene completion. Extensive experiments on the SemanticKITTI benchmark demonstrate that PatchScene achieves state-of-the-art performance across all standard metrics, surpassing previous approaches in both geometric accuracy and temporal consistency. Remarkably, the model trained on 20 m LiDAR ranges generalizes effectively to 50 m scenes without retraining, highlighting its strong scalability and generalization capability for real-world autonomous driving applications.

AI.tldr: PatchScene是一种基于扩散模型的大规模LiDAR场景补全框架，采用局部体素块扩散策略替代全局表示或密集体素网格，通过置信度引导的时空融合机制和利用激光雷达径向密度模式的环形 flow 扩散策略，实现高质量、空间无界的场景补全。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖全局潜在表示或密集体素网格，难以生成细粒度的局部几何信息，且在空间和时间尺度上难以保证一致性；同时无法实现无界场景补全（如从20m扩展到50m）。
AI.method: 提出三个核心创新：(1) 基于局部体素块的扩散范式，显式生成局部3D区域的几何细节；(2) 置信度引导的时空融合机制，整合重叠体素块和相邻帧；(3) Annular-Flow扩散策略，从近处到远处逐步传播高保真信息。
AI.result: 在SemanticKITTI基准数据集上达到最先进性能，跨越所有标准指标；在几何精度和时间一致性上均超越先前方法；在20m训练数据上无需重新训练即可泛化到50m场景。
AI.conclusion: PatchScene通过局部扩散范式和创新的时空融合机制成功实现了高质量、可扩展的场景补全，为自动驾驶应用提供了强大的 Scalability 和泛化能力。

relevance_reason: The paper addresses LiDAR scene completion (3D environment reconstruction from point clouds), which is a fundamentally different application domain from the research profile's focus on video-to-3D human motion generation. While both involve diffusion models in 3D contexts, the target applications are distinct: the profile requires cross-modal conversion from 2D video to 3D motion (with temporal and semantic alignment concerns), whereas PatchScene focuses on spatial scene completion with no video or motion generation components. The shared use of diffusion is not enough to establish relevance across these incompatible application domains.

title: Benchmarking Visual State Tracking in Multimodal Video Understanding
summary: Understanding a video requires more than recognizing isolated moments, as humans continuously track entities, states, and events over time. This capacity for visual state tracking is fundamental to video understanding, yet remains underexplored in current evaluations of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). We introduce Visual STAte Tracking benchmark (VSTAT), a video-based benchmark designed to diagnose visual state tracking in MLLMs. VSTAT consists of 834 clips drawn from both synthetic and real-world videos, paired with 1,500 questions that cannot be answered from any single frame or short segment, requiring continuous perception and integration of events across the entire video stream. Despite their strong performance on existing video benchmarks, we find that state-of-the-art MLLMs perform far below humans and only modestly above answer-prior baselines. To analyze this gap, we compare MLLMs' thinking traces with the underlying video stream to understand why and when MLLMs fail on VSTAT. We find that MLLMs reason and track correctly in text, but fail at visually perceiving the events they need to track. Finally, our preliminary evaluation suggests that recent agentic approaches, including MLLM-based video agents and coding agents, do not readily resolve these failures, still falling short on VSTAT.

AI.tldr: VSTAT是一个用于评估多模态大语言模型(MLLMs)视频视觉状态追踪能力的基准测试，包含834个视频片段和1500个问题。研究发现，尽管现有模型在现有基准上表现良好，但在VSTAT上远低于人类水平，仅略高于随机猜测基线。失败原因不是推理能力不足，而是视觉感知层面的缺陷。
AI.motivation: 当前视频理解评估忽略了连续状态追踪这一核心能力。现有的MLLM视频基准测试主要关注单帧或短片段理解，无法诊断模型是否具有跨时间的实体、状态和事件追踪能力。这导致即使在现有基准上表现优秀的模型，在真实视频理解任务上仍然存在明显的能力缺陷。
AI.method: 该研究构建了VSTAT基准测试，包含合成视频和真实世界视频共834个片段，每个问题都需要整合整个视频流的信息才能回答，而非仅凭单一帧或短片段。通过对比分析MLLMs的思维轨迹与底层视频流，诊断失败原因。同时评估了最新的agentic方法（包括MLLM视频智能体和编码智能体）的表现。
AI.result: 1) 最先进的MLLMs在VSTAT上表现远低于人类，仅略高于答案先验基线；2) 失败原因是视觉感知层面的缺陷，而非推理能力不足——模型能够正确进行文本推理和状态追踪，但在视觉感知需要追踪的事件时出现失败；3) 现有的agentic方法（包括视频智能体和编码智能体）仍然无法解决这些失败问题。
AI.conclusion: 视觉状态追踪是视频理解的基础能力，但当前MLLMs在此方面存在根本性缺陷。未来工作需要着重提升模型的视觉感知能力，而不仅仅是推理能力_agentic方法也未能有效解决此问题，需要新的技术突破来实现真正的连续视觉理解。该基准测试为诊断MLLMs的视频理解能力提供了重要工具。

relevance_reason: Wrong application domain. The paper is about video understanding and visual state tracking for multimodal LLMs, which serves evaluation/diagnostics purposes. The research profile needs video-to-motion conversion for computer graphics (specifically generating 3D motion from 2D video). While both involve video, the application domains are fundamentally different: video comprehension vs. video-to-motion generation. The methods also differ - the paper focuses on MLLM benchmarking rather than video generation models or cross-modal feature alignment pipelines needed for the specified research area.

title: GARDEN: Gravity-Aligned Reconstruction of Disentangled ENvironments from RGB images
summary: Converting multi-view RGB observations into simulation-ready 3D environments remains challenging because current reconstruction pipelines produce monolithic scene representations without explicit physical structure. They are typically defined up to an arbitrary global rotation and entangle rigid foreground objects with background geometry, which hinders stable physical interaction. Existing solutions often recover interactivity by replacing reconstructed objects with retrieved CAD assets, but this introduces a slow retrieval-and-replacement stage and weakens scene-specific geometric fidelity. We propose GARDEN, an RGB-only framework that reformulates reconstruction as physically-grounded scene factorization and outputs a structured hybrid scene representation. The key idea is to use gravity as a universal physical prior: we first align the reconstruction to a unified Gravity-View frame to resolve gauge ambiguity, then recover object-centric rigid meshes with accurate 6-DoF placement, and finally remove duplicate object geometry from the background through conditional 3D point classification. The resulting representation combines explicit rigid bodies with a decoupled background, enabling direct physics simulation while preserving visual realism. Experiments on both simulated and real multi-view scenes show that GARDEN improves object placement reliability, disentanglement quality, and rendering-simulation efficiency compared with retrieval-based baselines.

AI.tldr: Summary generation failed
AI.motivation: Motivation analysis unavailable
AI.method: Method extraction failed
AI.result: Result analysis unavailable
AI.conclusion: Conclusion extraction failed

relevance_reason: This paper focuses on 3D scene/environment reconstruction from RGB images, which is a completely different application domain from video-to-3D motion generation. Even though both deal with 3D understanding from 2D inputs, GARDEN addresses scene-level reconstruction for environmental understanding, not human motion generation. The research profile specifically needs methods for converting video to 3D human motion with temporal consistency and semantic alignment - these are distinct from scene reconstruction challenges.

title: Formalizing the Binding Problem
summary: Representations of the world, arguably, contain information about features (e.g. something is blue, something is a circle) but also information about which features are part of the same object (e.g. the circle is blue), which we call binding information. Any system with the ability to understand scenes with multiple objects must be able to solve the binding problem: it needs to know which features belong together. However, despite work showing that Vision Transformers (ViTs) know which patches belong together, it is not known whether current deep learning models learn to exhibit binding information, i.e., for features. We may believe that there is not much binding information, after all misattributing features to wrong objects is a common failure of ViT-based architectures, especially in scenes with objects sharing features. Here we formalize the binding problem with an information-theoretic approach, and introduce a probing method to measure binding information in model representations. We perform experiments on ViTs, measuring binding from different components of the architecture, such as the image summary token [CLS] or the spatial tokens. We use datasets with different binding challenges, such as feature sharing, occlusion, and natural features, while comparing the performance of several pre-trained ViTs. Overall, our research demonstrates binding as a key ingredient to strong visual recognition and reasoning.

AI.tldr: 本文研究视觉变换器(ViT)中特征绑定信息的学习问题，提出信息论形式的绑定问题形式化方法和探测方法，通过实验验证绑定是强视觉识别和推理的关键要素。
AI.motivation: 多对象场景理解需要解决绑定问题，即确定哪些特征属于同一对象。尽管现有研究表明ViT知道哪些补丁在一起，但尚不清楚深度学习模型是否学习了特征层面的绑定信息，且ViT常将特征错误归属到错误对象。
AI.method: 引入信息理论方法形式化绑定问题，设计探测方法来测量模型表示中的绑定信息。在不同绑定挑战的数据集上对多种预训练ViT进行实验，测量来自架构不同组件（如[CLS]标记或空间标记）的绑定信息。
AI.result: 实验表明绑定是关键 ingredients 到强视觉识别和推理，不同ViT架构组件表现不同，数据集涵盖特征共享、遮挡和自然特征等挑战。
AI.conclusion: 绑定作为关键要素对强视觉识别和推理至关重要，该工作为理解ViT及其他视觉模型的特征绑定机制提供了理论框架和方法工具。

relevance_reason: Completely irrelevant application domain. This paper studies feature binding in Vision Transformers for image-based visual recognitiontasks (determining which features belong to the same object in images). The research profile is about Computer Graphics: specifically video-to-3D-motion conversion, cross-modal alignment between 2D video and 3D motion, and 3D motion generation. While both deal with "features," the tasks are fundamentally different - the paper is about static image understanding, not motion generation or video-to-motion conversion. No overlap in application domain whatsoever.

title: Adaptive Causal Alignment for High-Confidence Adversarial Training
summary: Inverse adversarial training leverages high-confidence predictions to stabilize robust learning, yet we uncover a critical paradox: high confidence often stems from overfitting to non-causal background correlations rather than intrinsic object semantics. Our investigation reveals that visual context functions as a dual-natured signal, serving as either a necessary supportive prior or a spurious confounder. This insight renders existing blind suppression strategies flawed, as they inevitably lead to severe Feature Loss. To resolve this, we propose High-Confidence Causally Aligned Training (HICAT), a unified framework that establishes a Semantic Equilibrium. Operating on a ``Measure-Debias-Align'' pipeline, HICAT integrates a Learnable Background-Bias Estimator (LBBE) to adaptively diagnose context utility. Guided by this diagnosis, an Adaptive Debiasing mechanism performs surgical logit rectification, complemented by a geometrically grounded Foreground Logit Orthogonal Enhancement (FLOE) loss to enforce rigorous feature disentanglement. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet-1K demonstrate that HICAT consistently improves over matched baselines across diverse architectures (CNNs and ViTs) while significantly reducing the robust generalization gap.

AI.tldr: 提出HICAT框架，通过可学习的背景偏差估计器和前景对数正交增强损失，解决对抗训练中因背景过拟合导致的鲁棒性下降问题。
AI.motivation: 视觉上下文作为双刃剑（支持性先验或虚假混淆因素），需要自适应诊断其效用并精准解耦。
AI.method: 提出Measure-Debias-Align管道：LBBE可学习背景偏差估计器诊断上下文效用，适应性修正logits，结合FLOE前景对数正交增强损失实现严格的特征解纠缠。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10/100和ImageNet-1K上跨CNN和ViT架构一致提升基线性能，显著缩小鲁棒泛化差距。
AI.conclusion: 通过语义平衡和因果对齐实现了背景辅助与消除的有序切换，为可解释鲁棒学习提供了新范式。

relevance_reason: This paper is about adversarial training and robust neural networks for image classification, completely unrelated to Computer Graphics or Video-to-Motion conversion. It addresses background bias in image classification tasks on CIFAR/ImageNet datasets, not cross-modal alignment between 2D video and 3D motion, temporal consistency, or semantic text-to-motion alignment. The application domain is entirely different - it's about improving robustness in standard image classification, not generating 3D motion from video sources.

title: Demo2Tutorial: From Human Experience to Multimodal Software Tutorials
summary: Human experience in digital environments offers a vast, underexplored resource of authentic, untrimmed interactions that contain rich procedural knowledge. We introduce Demo2Tutorial, a framework that transforms this experience captured via screen recordings and interaction logs into structured, multimodal software tutorials for teaching both humans and agents. Demo2Tutorial first collects human experience via a dedicated recorder, then parses raw experience using a multimodal Action Parser to reconstruct perception, action, and intent. A Step Planner then abstracts these steps into hierarchical task graphs representing goals and steps. Finally, a Tutorial Composer transforms the parsed experience into structured, reusable image-text instructions. We evaluate the tutorial generation quality on a new benchmark derived from official software documentation. We further demonstrate that this distilled representation benefits (i) human learning, by automatically generating multimodal tutorials, and (ii) agent learning, by improving downstream GUI-agent planning and generalization. Experiments show Demo2Tutorial produces high-quality tutorials that surpass human-authored ones and significantly outperform baseline methods, while enabling both faster human task completion and improved GUI agent planning, demonstrating that structured tutorials distilled from human experience can serve as effective knowledge representations for advancing both human learning and agent capabilities. Code and data will be available at https://github.com/showlab/Demo2Tutorial.

AI.tldr: Demo2Tutorial是一个将人类数字操作经验（屏幕录制+交互日志）自动转换为结构化多模态软件教程的框架，可同时服务于人类学习和AI代理训练。
AI.motivation: 人类在数字环境中的操作经验包含丰富的程序性知识，但目前未被充分利用。现有的软件教程多为预编辑内容，缺乏灵活性和个性化，且难以被AI代理利用。需要一种方法从真实的人类交互中自动提取和结构化知识。
AI.method: 1)Recorder收集人类体验；2)多模态Action Parser解析原始经验，重建感知、行动和意图；3)Step Planner将步骤抽象为层次化任务图；4)Tutorial Composer将解析经验转化为结构化图像-文本指令
AI.result: 新benchmark评估表明：生成的教程质量超越人类-authored教程，显著优于基线方法；人类学习者任务完成速度更快；下游GUI代理规划能力和泛化性能明显提升。
AI.conclusion: 从人类真实经验中蒸馏的结构化教程是一种有效的知识表示，可同时促进人类学习和AI代理能力，代表了人机协作知识提取的新方向。

relevance_reason: This paper addresses a completely different application domain. It focuses on creating software tutorials from screen recordings to serve human learners and AI agents, whereas the research profile is about Computer Graphics specifically for video-to-3D-motion generation. While both involve analyzing video/screen content, the end goal is entirely different: converting human operations into instructional tutorials vs. extracting 3D motion from video for graphics/animation. There is no alignment with the core pain points of cross-modal video-to-motion conversion, temporal consistency, or 3D motion generation using methods like Seedance.

title: VLESA: Vision-Language Embodied Safety Agent for Human Activity Monitoring
summary: As AI systems increasingly assist humans in physical tasks, ensuring safety becomes paramount -- physical actions carry immediate and irreversible consequences that digital errors do not. We introduce the Vision-Language Embodied Safety Agent (VLESA), a framework that monitors human activities from egocentric video and triggers real-time safety interventions when dangerous actions are predicted. VLESA addresses intent-dependent safety where identical actions can be safe or dangerous depending on context. A dataset pairing egocentric frames with goal-conditioned safety annotations is introduced, enabling a goal-conditioned safety Q-filter trained via GRPO that evaluates actions with respect to inferred intent without retraining. On top of that, an intent-action prediction agent is proposed to jointly infer goals and predict future actions from video. On the ASIMOV-2.0 benchmark, VLESA achieves higher intervention accuracy at the exact ground-truth frame compared to baselines, while the GRPO-trained Q-filter improves action safety by over 41 percentage points through goal-conditioned constrained decoding. Code is available at https://github.com/HanjiangHu/VLESA.

AI.tldr: VLESA是一个视觉语言具身安全智能体，通过监控第一人称视角视频来预测危险动作并在必要时触发实时安全干预。
AI.motivation: 随着AI系统越来越多地协助人类完成物理任务，确保安全问题变得至关重要——物理动作具有即时且不可逆的后果，而相同的动作可能根据意图上下文是安全或危险的。
AI.method: 提出了三个核心组件：1）构建了带有目标条件安全标注的第一人称视角数据集；2）通过GRPO训练了目标条件安全Q过滤器，可在不重训练的情况下评估动作安全性；3）提出了意图-动作预测智能体，用于从视频中联合推断目标并预测未来动作。
AI.result: 在ASIMOV-2.0基准上，VLESA在精确的真实帧位置实现了更高的干预准确率，且通过目标条件约束解码，GRPO训练的Q过滤器将动作安全性提升了超过41个百分点。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为具身AI安全提供了新的框架思路，通过结合意图推断与安全过滤，在保持更高干预精度同时显著提升动作安全性。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain. The research profile focuses on Computer Graphics - specifically video-to-3D motion generation using video generation models like Wanxiang/Seedance. The paper is about embodied AI safety monitoring - using first-person video to detect dangerous human actions and trigger safety interventions. Even though both deal with video, this is surveillance/action-understanding rather than content generation. The methods (GRPO training, intent-action prediction, safety Q-filters) are unrelated to cross-modal video-to-motion generation, temporal consistency, or visual quality assessment for motion synthesis.

title: Video-Mirai: Autoregressive Video Diffusion Models Need Foresight
summary: Causal video generators must predict from the past, but they need not learn only from it. In streaming autoregressive video diffusion, each emitted segment becomes a commitment that future segments must preserve. Standard training, however, only asks each causal state to explain the present. This creates what we call a representation-level planning gap: states that fit the current segment may discard identity, layout, and motion information needed for a consistent future. We introduce Video-Mirai, a training-only method that closes this gap without changing causal inference: the generator rolls out causally, a frozen foresight encoder reads the completed rollout non-causally, and a lightweight predictor distills the resulting stopped-gradient targets into causal states. Future frames supervise representations, never generator inputs. At inference, the encoder and predictor are discarded, leaving the original architecture, per-step FLOPs, and KV-cache behavior unchanged. Video-Mirai improves a strong Causal-Forcing baseline on 5-second VBench from 83.8 to 84.6 in terms of Total Score. On 30-second rollouts beyond the training horizon, subject consistency improves from 84.9 to 88.5 and background consistency from 90.2 to 91.9. Ablations identify future-conditioned targets as the key ingredient, and probes show that future frames become more decodable from current features. Causality should constrain inference, not representation supervision. Our study highlights that visual autoregressive models need foresight. Project page: https://y0uroy.github.io/Video-Mirai.

AI.tldr: Video-Mirai是一种训练方法，通过引入前向观察encoder和轻量级预测器，将未来帧监督信号蒸馏到因果状态表示中，从而关闭自回归视频生成中的"表示级规划差距"，使模型能够在保持原有架构和计算效率的前提下，提高长期 rollout 的主体和背景一致性。
AI.motivation: 问题背景：流式自回归视频扩散中，每个发出的片段成为未来片段必须保留的承诺，但标准训练只要求每个因果状态解释当前片段，这导致适合当前片段的状态可能丢弃了保持一致性未来所需的身份、布局和运动信息。核心贡献：提出"表示级规划差距"(representation-level planning gap)这一概念，并设计训练方法在不改变推理架构的情况下关闭该差距。
AI.method: Video-Mirai采用三阶段训练：(1)基础生成器按因果方式rollout；(2)冻结的前瞻encoder非因果读取完整rollout；(3)轻量级预测器将停止梯度的目标蒸馏到因果状态中。未来帧监督表示，从不监督generator输入。推理时丢弃encoder和预测器，保持原架构、单步FLOPs和KV-cache行为不变。
AI.result: 在VBench 5秒测试集上，总分从83.8提升至84.6；在超出训练范围的30秒rollout上，主体一致性从84.9提升至88.5，背景一致性从90.2提升至91.9。消融实验确认未来条件目标是关键成分；探针实验显示未来帧可从当前特征中更易解码。
AI.conclusion: 因果性应约束推理而非表示监督。研究表明视觉自回归模型需要前瞻能力。当前方法在保持原架构和计算效率的同时有效提升了长期视频一致性，为视频生成模型提供新训练范式。

relevance_reason: The paper addresses temporal consistency in VIDEO GENERATION (video-to-video), which is fundamentally different from the research profile's focus on VIDEO-TO-MOTION CONVERSION (2D video to 3D motion). Both involve video but deal with entirely different output modalities - one generates frames, the other generates 3D body movements. This is similar to how medical image generation differs from human motion generation. No mention of 3D pose, motion capture, or cross-modal video-to-motion tasks. Only shares superficial similarity in dealing with temporal consistency.

title: AAD-1: Asymmetric Adversarial Distillation for One-Step Autoregressive Video Generation
summary: We present AAD-1, an Asymmetric Adversarial Distillation framework for One-step autoregressive image-to-video generation. State-of-the-art methods adopt adversarial distillation but suffer from motion collapse and training instability, resulting in static videos. AAD-1 addresses these challenges through two key designs in architecture and training strategy. Our key architectural insight is to break the symmetry between generator and discriminator. While the generator remains causal to preserve autoregressive sampling capability, the discriminator attends bidirectionally over the full spatiotemporal context and produces a single holistic realism score for the entire video sequence. This asymmetric design enables the discriminator to effectively detect global temporal failures and long-range drift that cause motion collapse in autoregressive generation. To stabilize training, we introduce a phased strategy that first uses distribution matching to bootstrap a stable one-step generator, providing a warm-up phase that brings the student distribution closer to the teacher before adversarial distillation begins. Extensive experiments on VBench demonstrate that AAD-1 achieves state-of-the-art performance in one-step autoregressive video generation.

AI.tldr: AAD-1是一种用于单步自回归图像到视频生成的非对称对抗蒸馏框架，通过打破生成器和判别器之间的对称性设计，以及分阶段训练策略来解决运动崩溃和训练不稳定问题，在VBench上实现最先进的单步自回归视频生成性能。
AI.motivation: 当前最先进的自回归图像到视频生成方法采用对抗蒸馏，但存在运动崩溃（生成静态视频）和训练不稳定的显著问题，导致模型无法产生动态连贯的视频内容。
AI.method: 提出两个关键设计：1) 非对称架构——保持生成器的因果结构以保留自回归采样能力，同时让判别器双向Attend全场域时空上下文并生成单一整体真实度评分，使判别器能够有效检测全局时间失败和长距离漂移；2) 分阶段训练策略——先使用分布匹配 bootstrapping 稳定单步生成器作为预热阶段，再进行对抗蒸馏。
AI.result: 在VBench上的广泛实验表明，AAD-1在单步自回归视频生成任务中达到最先进的性能。
AI.conclusion: AAD-1通过非对称设计和分阶段训练策略有效解决了自回归生成中的运动崩溃和训练不稳定问题，为单步自回归视频生成提供了新的有效方案。

relevance_reason: While both papers deal with video/motion topics in Computer Graphics, they address completely different application domains. The research profile focuses on VIDEO-TO-MOTION CONVERSION (extracting/generating 3D human motion from video input), while this paper focuses on IMAGE-TO-VIDEO GENERATION (generating video frames from static images). These are fundamentally different tasks: one is about human motion generation from video signals, the other is about visual content synthesis. The paper uses similar diffusion methods but targets wrong application domain - it's about creating video content, not extracting or aligning 3D motion representations. Temporal consistency in the paper refers to video frame coherence, not motion sequence consistency.

title: NewtPhys: Do Foundation Models Understand Newtonian Physics?
summary: Previous work has evaluated physics reasoning in foundation models using synthetic or semi-synthetic scenes and visual question-answering tasks. However, these benchmarks emphasize high-level events and lack the visual fidelity required to assess true low-level Newtonian understanding. We introduce NewtPhys, a 4D physically annotated dataset built from multiview images of real-world scenes with physics-grounded simulations. The dataset provides dense, fine-grained annotations across timesteps -- including 3D forces and amodal per-pixel quantities covering physics, tracking, semantics and geometry -- bridging the gap between simplistic synthetic setups and realistic visual complexity. Using NewtPhys, we systematically evaluate 56 VLMs, including 54 open-weight models and 2 closed-source frontier models, and 10 VFMs and reveal limitations in low-level physics reasoning. Beyond benchmarking, our dataset enables future research in physics-grounded vision and the development of next-generation physics-aware evaluations. Code and datasets are available at https://astra-vision.github.io/NewtPhys.

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了NewtPhys，一个基于真实场景多视图图像构建的4D物理标注数据集，用于评估视觉语言模型和视觉foundation模型的低层次牛顿物理推理能力。
AI.motivation: 先前的工作使用合成或半合成场景进行物理推理评估，缺乏真实世界的视觉保真度，无法评估真正的低层次牛顿物理理解能力。
AI.method: 构建了包含密集细粒度标注的4D物理数据集（涵盖3D力、模态前景、语义和几何信息），系统评估了56个VLM（含54个开源和2个闭源模型）及10个VFM。
AI.result: 揭示了当前模型在低层次物理推理方面的局限性。
AI.conclusion: NewtPhys数据集为未来物理 grounding 的视觉研究和下一代物理感知评估奠定了基础。

relevance_reason: Application domain mismatch: The research profile targets Computer Graphics specifically focused on video-to-3D motion generation, while this paper (NewtPhys) is about evaluating physics reasoning capabilities of VLM/VFMs - a fundamentally different field. Although both involve VLMs, they serve completely different purposes: the profile uses VLMs for quality assessment in video-to-motion pipelines, while NewtPhys uses VLMs to test physics understanding. This paper does not address any of the stated pain points (cross-modal alignment, temporal consistency, quality control, or semantic alignment) and has no direct relevance to computer graphics motion generation research.

title: PixVOD: Pixel-Distributed Direct Visual Odometry and Depth Estimation
summary:
Images composed of 2D pixel arrays are the standard input to computer vision algorithms, yet many underlying computations can be distributed across pixels. Transmitting raw, redundant, and noisy pixel data off the sensor remains inefficient, motivating a shift toward focal-plane sensor-processors that perform a significant part of the computation directly within each pixel. We envision pixels synthesizing higher-level signals locally, reducing downstream load, and providing richer inputs for higher-level vision tasks.
  We propose a fully parallelizable form of visual odometry and depth estimation across pixels, where sensor-processors exchange information through Gaussian Belief Propagation (GBP) to achieve consensus about camera motion and infer depth from per-pixel photometric observations and a surface normal prior. To maintain geometric stability during optimization, we introduce a keyframe-like anchoring mechanism that regulates the effective baseline between frames, enabling consistent motion and depth updates. Our method is evaluated on realistic datasets, demonstrating the feasibility of GBP-based pixel-level distributed odometry and depth estimation with keyframe anchoring on-sensor. Project Page: https://www.shinjeongkim.com/pixvod/

AI.tldr: 提出一种在焦平面传感器处理器上实现的全并行视觉里程计和深度估计方法，通过高斯置信传播(GBP)在像素间交换信息，结合关键帧锚定机制实现相机运动共识和深度推断。
AI.motivation: 传统图像处理将原始像素数据传输到后端计算效率低下，存在冗余和噪声数据传输问题，需要转向在焦平面传感器处理器上进行像素级分布式计算，减少下游负载。
AI.method: 提出完全并行化的视觉里程计和深度估计方法：传感器处理器通过高斯置信传播(GBP)在像素间交换信息，从每像素光度观测和表面法线先验达成相机运动共识并推断深度；引入类似关键帧的锚定机制调节帧间有效基线，保持几何稳定性。
AI.result: 在现实数据集上验证了基于GBP的像素级分布式里程计和深度估计以及传感器上关键帧锚定的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法实现了在传感器上直接进行分布式视觉里程计和深度估计，为高效嵌入式视觉处理提供了新途径。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain - Paper is about visual odometry and depth estimation (computer vision/sensor processing), while profile requires video-to-3D motion generation in computer graphics. Uses unrelated methods (GBP for camera tracking vs video/motion generation models). No pain point alignment.

title: Exploring Easy Boosts for Lidar Semantic Scene Completion
summary: This paper investigates "free lunch" strategies to boost the performance of lidar semantic scene completion (SSC) without requiring complex architectural redesigns. We first demonstrate that endowing input point clouds with semantic pseudo-labels from off-the-shelf segmentors significantly improves the performance of existing architectures. By evaluating these models against an oracle, we establish that high-quality semantic priors are a primary driver of mIoU gains. Furthermore, we equip the input lidar scan with visibility information that distinguishes between empty and unknown spaces, which provides a secondary performance boost across the tested architectures. Using these simple enhancements, we observe that older models remain competitive with state-of-the-art systems, and can even outperform them. Our code is available at https://github.com/astra-vision/SSC-Priors.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需复杂网络架构重新设计即可提升LiDAR语义场景补全(SSC)性能的"免费午餐"策略。通过为输入点云添加语义伪标签和可见性信息两个简单增强，使旧模型也能达到甚至超越当前最先进水平。
AI.motivation: 现有LiDAR语义场景补全方法需要复杂的网络架构重新设计才能提升性能，本文旨在探索是否存在简单有效的改进策略，在不修改核心架构的情况下显著提升模型性能。
AI.method: 提出了两种简单的输入增强策略：1)使用现成分割器的语义伪标签作为输入先验；2)为激光雷达扫描添加可见性信息以区分空区域和未知区域。通过oracle实验验证高质量语义先验是mIoU提升的主要驱动力。
AI.result: 这两种简单增强在多个测试架构上均带来显著性能提升。旧模型在加入这些增强后，其性能可与最先进的系统相媲美，甚至超越它们。
AI.conclusion: 语义先验和可见性信息是提升LiDAR语义场景补全性能的关键因素。这表明通过简单的输入增强即可在不改变模型架构的情况下获得显著收益，为该领域提供了实用且高效的改进方向。

relevance_reason: This paper is about LiDAR semantic scene completion for autonomous vehicles/robotics perception, which is in a completely different application domain than the research profile focused on Computer Graphics (video-to-motion generation, text-to-motion generation). Even though both involve 3D data, LiDAR point cloud processing for scene understanding has no overlap with 2D video-to-3D motion conversion, visual quality assessment for generated videos, or cross-modal alignment between video/text and 3D motion. These are entirely different research fields.

title: SimuScene: Simulation-Ready Compositional 3D Scene Reconstruction from a Single Image
summary: Reconstructing interactive, simulation-ready 3D scenes from a single image is a critical bottleneck for robotic manipulation. While recent single-image lifters recover plausible per-object shapes, composing them yields scenes that collapse under physical simulation due to interpenetrating, hovering, or sinking objects. Existing physics-aware methods address this strictly as a post-hoc layout correction, leaving the underlying geometric errors unresolved. To address this, we introduce SimuScene, a compositional 3D reconstruction pipeline that puts physics in the loop of shape and layout estimation. Rather than using physics merely for layout cleanup, we utilize the physics engine as a diagnostic measurement tool during the generative process itself. By diagnostically simulating reconstructed objects under gravity, we convert penetration and support failures into quantitative correction signals that drive gravity-axis stretching and amodal shape resampling. This physics-informed feedback loop mitigates accumulated reconstruction errors and produces a stable, simulation-ready compositional 3D scene. Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance on physical stability and geometric alignment benchmarks. We further highlight SimuScene's utility by deploying reconstructed environments in humanoid control and robot-arm manipulation tasks.

AI.tldr: 本文提出SimuScene，一种将物理引擎集成到3D场景重建流程中的新方法，解决单图像重建中物体穿插、悬空和下沉导致的物理仿真实崩溃问题。
AI.motivation: 单图像3D重建是机器人操作的关键瓶颈，现有单图像提升器虽能恢复合理的单个物体形状，但组合成的场景在物理仿真下会崩溃，因为存在物体间穿插、悬空或下沉等问题。现有物理感知方法仅作为后处理布局修正，未解决根本的几何误差。
AI.method: 提出SimuScene组合3D重建管道，将物理置于形状和布局估计的循环中。核心创新是将物理引擎作为诊断测量工具，而非仅用于布局清理。通过对重建对象进行诊断性仿真（施加重力），将穿透和支持失败转化为定量校正信号，驱动重力轴拉伸和非模态形状重采样。
AI.result: 在物理稳定性和几何对齐基准上实现了最先进的性能，并展示了在人形机器人控制和机械臂操作任务中的实用性。
AI.conclusion: 物理信息反馈循环能够减轻累积重建误差，产生稳定且仿真就绪的组合3D场景，为机器人操作提供了可靠的3D环境重建方案。

relevance_reason: Completely different application domain. The research profile focuses on VIDEO-TO-MOTION conversion with temporal consistency and text-to-motion alignment, while SimuScene addresses SINGLE IMAGE TO 3D SCENE RECONSTRUCTION with physics simulation for static scenes. Although both fields are in computer graphics, they deal with fundamentally different problems: dynamic motion generation vs static scene reconstruction. No overlap in methods or goals - this paper would not help address any of the specified pain points.
